 `./lib/campaignKolService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→export interface CampaignKOL {
     4→  id: string;
     5→  campaign_id: string;
     6→  master_kol_id: string;
     7→  hh_status: 'Curated' | 'Contacted' | 'Interested' | 'Onboarded' | 'Concluded' | null;
     8→  client_status?: 'Rejected' | 'Preferred' | null;
     9→  notes?: string | null;
    10→  allocated_budget?: number | null;
    11→  budget_type?: 'Token' | 'Fiat' | 'WL' | null;
    12→  paid?: number | null;
    13→  wallet?: string | null;
    14→  hidden?: boolean | null;
    15→  created_at?: string | null;
    16→  updated_at?: string | null;
    17→}
    18→
    19→export interface CampaignKOLWithDetails extends CampaignKOL {
    20→  master_kol: {
    21→    id: string;
    22→    name: string;
    23→    link?: string | null;
    24→    platform?: string[] | null;
    25→    followers?: number | null;
    26→    region?: string | null;
    27→    community?: boolean | null;
    28→    content_type?: string[] | null;
    29→    creator_type?: string[] | null;
    30→    niche?: string[] | null;
    31→    pricing?: string | null;
    32→    tier?: string | null;
    33→    rating?: number | null;
    34→    group_chat?: boolean | null;
    35→    description?: string | null;
    36→    wallet?: string | null;
    37→  };
    38→}
    39→
    40→export class CampaignKOLService {
    41→  static async getCampaignKOLs(campaignId: string): Promise<CampaignKOLWithDetails[]> {
    42→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    43→      .from('campaign_kols')
    44→      .select(`
    45→        *,
    46→        master_kol:master_kols(*)
    47→      `)
    48→      .eq('campaign_id', campaignId)
    49→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    50→
    51→    if (error) {
    52→      console.error('Error fetching campaign KOLs:', error);
    53→      throw new Error('Failed to fetch campaign KOLs');
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    return (data || []) as CampaignKOLWithDetails[];
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  static async addCampaignKOL(campaignId: string, masterKolId: string, hhStatus: CampaignKOL['hh_status'], notes?: string): Promise<CampaignKOL> {
    60→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    61→      .from('campaign_kols')
    62→      .insert({
    63→        campaign_id: campaignId,
    64→        master_kol_id: masterKolId,
    65→        hh_status: hhStatus,
    66→        notes: notes || null
    67→      })
    68→      .select()
    69→      .single();
    70→
    71→    if (error) {
    72→      console.error('Error adding campaign KOL:', error);
    73→      throw new Error('Failed to add campaign KOL');
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    return data as CampaignKOL;
    77→  }
    78→
    79→  static async updateCampaignKOL(id: string, updates: Partial<CampaignKOL>): Promise<CampaignKOL> {
    80→    const { data, error } = await supabase
    81→      .from('campaign_kols')
    82→      .update(updates)
    83→      .eq('id', id)
    84→      .select()
    85→      .single();
    86→
    87→    if (error) {
    88→      console.error('Error updating campaign KOL:', error);
    89→      throw new Error('Failed to update campaign KOL');
    90→    }
    91→
    92→    return data as CampaignKOL;
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  static async deleteCampaignKOL(id: string): Promise<void> {
    96→    const { error } = await supabase
    97→      .from('campaign_kols')
    98→      .delete()
    99→      .eq('id', id);
   100→
   101→    if (error) {
   102→      console.error('Error deleting campaign KOL:', error);
   103→      throw new Error('Failed to delete campaign KOL');
   104→    }
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  static async getAvailableKOLs(campaignId: string): Promise<any[]> {
   108→    // First get the IDs of KOLs already assigned to this campaign
   109→    const { data: assignedKOLs, error: assignedError } = await supabase
   110→      .from('campaign_kols')
   111→      .select('master_kol_id')
   112→      .eq('campaign_id', campaignId);
   113→
   114→    if (assignedError) {
   115→      console.error('Error fetching assigned KOLs:', assignedError);
   116→      throw new Error('Failed to fetch assigned KOLs');
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    // Get the IDs of assigned KOLs
   120→    const assignedIds = assignedKOLs?.map(kol => kol.master_kol_id) || [];
   121→
   122→    // If no KOLs are assigned, return all non-archived KOLs
   123→    if (assignedIds.length === 0) {
   124→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   125→        .from('master_kols')
   126→        .select('*')
   127→        .is('archived_at', null)
   128→        .order('name');
   129→
   130→      if (error) {
   131→        console.error('Error fetching all KOLs:', error);
   132→        throw new Error('Failed to fetch KOLs');
   133→      }
   134→
   135→      return data || [];
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    // Get all non-archived KOLs that are not in the assigned list
   139→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   140→      .from('master_kols')
   141→      .select('*')
   142→      .is('archived_at', null)
   143→      .not('id', 'in', `(${assignedIds.join(',')})`)
   144→      .order('name');
   145→
   146→    if (error) {
   147→      console.error('Error fetching available KOLs:', error);
   148→      throw new Error('Failed to fetch available KOLs');
   149→    }
   150→
   151→    return data || [];
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  static getHHStatusOptions(): CampaignKOL['hh_status'][] {
   155→    return ['Curated', 'Contacted', 'Interested', 'Onboarded', 'Concluded'];
   156→  }
   157→
   158→  static getClientStatusOptions(): ('Rejected' | 'Preferred')[] {
   159→    return ['Rejected', 'Preferred'];
   160→  }
   161→}
```

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Campaign = Database['public']['Tables']['campaigns']['Row'];
     6→type CampaignBudgetAllocation = Database['public']['Tables']['campaign_budget_allocations']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface CampaignWithDetails extends Campaign {
     9→  client_name?: string;
    10→  client_email?: string;
    11→  client_logo_url?: string | null;
    12→  budget_allocations?: CampaignBudgetAllocation[];
    13→  total_allocated?: number;
    14→  share_creator_type?: boolean;
    15→  slug?: string | null;
    16→}
    17→
    18→export class CampaignService {
    19→  /**
    20→   * Get campaigns based on user role (RLS enforced at database level)
    21→   * - Admins: See all campaigns
    22→   * - Members: See campaigns they created (created_by) or are assigned to (manager)
    23→   */
    24→  static async getCampaignsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string, supabaseClient?: any): Promise<CampaignWithDetails[]> {
    25→    try {
    26→      const client = supabaseClient || supabase;
    27→
    28→      // RLS policies handle access control:
    29→      // - Admins: can see all campaigns
    30→      // - Members: can see campaigns they created (created_by) or are assigned to (manager)
    31→      // Filter out archived campaigns
    32→      const { data: campaigns, error } = await client
    33→        .from('campaigns')
    34→        .select(`
    35→          *,
    36→          clients!campaigns_client_id_fkey(name, email, logo_url),
    37→          campaign_budget_allocations(*)
    38→        `)
    39→        .is('archived_at', null)
    40→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    41→
    42→      if (error) throw error;
    43→
    44→      return campaigns?.map(campaign => ({
    45→        ...campaign,
    46→        client_name: (campaign.clients as any)?.name,
    47→        client_email: (campaign.clients as any)?.email,
    48→        client_logo_url: (campaign.clients as any)?.logo_url,
    49→        budget_allocations: campaign.campaign_budget_allocations || [],
    50→        total_allocated: campaign.campaign_budget_allocations?.reduce((sum: number, allocation: any) => sum + allocation.allocated_budget, 0) || 0,
    51→      })) || [];
    52→    } catch (error) {
    53→      console.error('Error fetching campaigns:', error);
    54→      throw error;
    55→    }
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  /**
    59→   * Get a single campaign by ID with budget allocations
    60→   */
    61→  static async getCampaignById(campaignId: string, supabaseClient?: any): Promise<CampaignWithDetails | null> {
    62→    try {
    63→      const client = supabaseClient || supabase;
    64→      const { data: campaign, error } = await client
    65→        .from('campaigns')
    66→        .select(`
    67→          *,
    68→          clients!campaigns_client_id_fkey(name, email, logo_url),
    69→          campaign_budget_allocations(*)
    70→        `)
    71→        .eq('id', campaignId)
    72→        .single();
    73→
    74→      if (error) throw error;
    75→      if (!campaign) return null;
    76→
    77→      return {
    78→        ...campaign,
    79→        client_name: (campaign.clients as any)?.name,
    80→        client_email: (campaign.clients as any)?.email,
    81→        client_logo_url: (campaign.clients as any)?.logo_url,
    82→        budget_allocations: campaign.campaign_budget_allocations || [],
    83→        total_allocated: campaign.campaign_budget_allocations?.reduce((sum: number, allocation: any) => sum + allocation.allocated_budget, 0) || 0,
    84→
    85→      };
    86→    } catch (error) {
    87→      console.error('Error fetching campaign:', error);

    89→    }
    90→  }
```

```text
   350→      const portalAuth = localStorage.getItem('portal_global_auth');
   351→      if (portalAuth) {

   353→        const now = Date.now();
   354→
   355→        // Check if portal auth is still valid and email matches
   356→        if (now - portalTimestamp < CACHE_DURATION) {
   357→          const portalEmailLower = portalEmail?.toLowerCase();
   358→          const clientEmailLower = clientEmail.toLowerCase();
   359→          const portalClientEmailLower = portalClientEmail?.toLowerCase();
   360→
   361→          // If portal was authenticated with the same client email, auto-authenticate
   362→          if (portalEmail && (portalEmailLower === clientEmailLower || portalClientEmailLower === clientEmailLower)) {
   363→            setEmail(portalEmail);
   364→            setIsAuthenticated(true);
   365→            return;
   366→          }
   367→        }
   368→      }
   369→
   370→      const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey);
   371→      if (cached) {
   372→        const { email: cachedEmail, timestamp } = JSON.parse(cached);
   373→        const now = Date.now();
   374→
   375→        // Check if cache is still valid (within 24 hours)
   376→        if (now - timestamp < CACHE_DURATION) {
   377→          // Verify the cached email matches client email, is in approved emails, or has same domain
   378→          const cachedEmailLower = cachedEmail?.toLowerCase();
   379→          const clientEmailLower = clientEmail.toLowerCase();
   380→          const isClientEmail = cachedEmailLower === clientEmailLower;
   381→          const isApprovedEmail = approvedEmails.some(approved => approved.toLowerCase() === cachedEmailLower);
   382→
   383→          // Check if email has the same domain as client email
   384→          const getEmailDomain = (email: string) => email.split('@')[1];
   385→          const cachedDomain = cachedEmailLower ? getEmailDomain(cachedEmailLower) : null;
   386→          const clientDomain = getEmailDomain(clientEmailLower);
   387→          const isSameDomain = cachedDomain && clientDomain && cachedDomain === clientDomain;
   388→          const isApprovedDomain = approvedDomains.some(domain =>
   389→            cachedDomain?.toLowerCase() === domain.toLowerCase()
   390→          );
   391→
   392→          if (cachedEmail && (isClientEmail || isApprovedEmail || isSameDomain || isApprovedDomain)) {
   393→            setEmail(cachedEmail);
   394→            setIsAuthenticated(true);
   395→            return;
   396→          }
   397→        }
   398→
   399→        // Cache expired or invalid, remove it
   400→        localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);
   401→      }
   402→    } catch (error) {
   403→      console.error('Error checking cached auth:', error);
   404→      localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);

   457→      if (campaignError) {

   459→        throw new Error('Campaign not found or access denied');
   460→      }
   461→
   462→      if (!campaignData?.client_id) {
   463→        throw new Error('Campaign has no associated client');
   464→      }
   465→
   466→      // Store the campaign UUID for logging
   467→      setCampaignUuid(campaignData.id);

   469→      // Store approved emails and domains from campaign
   470→      setApprovedEmails((campaignData.approved_emails as string[]) || []);
   471→      setApprovedDomains((campaignData.approved_domains as string[]) || []);
   472→
   473→      const { data: clientData, error: clientError } = await supabasePublic
   474→        .from('clients')
   475→        .select('email, approved_domains')
   476→        .eq('id', campaignData.client_id)
   477→        .single();
   478→
   479→      if (clientError) {
   480→        console.error('Client fetch error:', clientError);
   481→        throw new Error('Client information not found');
   482→      }
   483→
   484→      if (!clientData?.email) {
   485→        throw new Error('Client has no email address configured');
   486→      }
   487→
   488→      setClientEmail(clientData.email);
   489→
   490→      // Merge client-level approved domains with campaign-level
   491→      const clientDomains = (clientData.approved_domains as string[]) || [];
   492→      if (clientDomains.length > 0) {
   493→        setApprovedDomains(prev => {
   494→          const merged = new Set([...prev, ...clientDomains]);
   495→          return Array.from(merged);
   496→        });
   497→      }
   498→    } catch (e: any) {
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-11 18:34:45Z)**_

Now I have a comprehensive picture of all three areas. Let me compile the full findings.

---

Here is a complete breakdown of everything you asked about:

---

## 1. Form Responses Data

### `form_responses` table (live DB columns)

| Column | Type | Nullable |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | uuid | NO |
| `form_id` | uuid | NO |
| `response_data` | **jsonb** | NO |
| `submitted_by_email` | text | YES |
| `submitted_by_name` | text | YES |
| `submitted_at` | timestamptz | YES |
| `client_id` | uuid | YES (FK to clients) |

**Key point:** The column is named `response_data` (not `responses`). It is **JSONB**. The TypeScript interface calls it `response_data: Record<string, any>` as well.

### Shape of `response_data`
The JSONB object is keyed by **`field_id` (UUID strings)** from `form_fields`, not by field labels. Values vary by field type:
- `text / email / number / date / textarea` → plain string: `{ "field-uuid": "some text" }`
- `checkbox` → array of strings: `{ "field-uuid": ["Option A", "Option C"] }`
- `select / radio` → plain string
- `allow_multiple` fields → array of strings (multiple answers)
- Yes/No reason fields → companion key: `{ "field-uuid": "yes", "field-uuid_reason": "Because..." }`
- File attachment fields → companion key: `{ "field-uuid": "text value", "field-uuid_attachments": ["https://...url"] }`

### `form_fields` table columns (live DB)
`id`, `form_id`, `field_type` (enum), `label`, `required`, `options` (jsonb - array of strings), `display_order`, `created_at`, `page_number`, `allow_multiple`, `include_other`, `allow_attachments`, `require_yes_reason`, `require_no_reason`, `is_yes_no_dropdown`

### `forms` table columns (live DB)
`id`, `user_id`, `name`, `description`, `status` (enum: draft/published/closed), `created_at`, `updated_at`, `enable_thank_you_page`, `thank_you_message`, `slug`, `archived_at`, `subdomain_enabled`, `subdomain_url`

Note: `database.types.ts` is **stale** — it is missing `enable_thank_you_page`, `thank_you_message`, `archived_at`, `slug`, `subdomain_enabled`, `subdomain_url` from forms.

### How form responses are fetched
In `lib/formService.ts`, `getResponses(formId)` runs:
```ts
supabase.from('form_responses')
  .select('*, client:clients!form_responses_client_id_fkey(name)')
  .eq('form_id', formId)
  .order('submitted_at', { ascending: false })
```
Returns `FormResponse[]` with `response_data` (the JSONB blob) and a joined `client_name`.

To **display** a response, you must also fetch the form's fields so you can map `field_id -> label`. The CSV export in `formService.ts` demonstrates this pattern:
```ts
form.fields.map(field => {
  const value = response.response_data[field.id];
  if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.join(', ');
  return value || '';
})
```

### Onboarding form (`holo-hive-onboarding`)
- Identified by `slug = 'holo-hive-onboarding'` (hardcoded in clients page as `ONBOARDING_FORM_SLUG`)
- The form is **fully dynamic** — questions are whatever fields exist in `form_fields` for that form. There is no hardcoded question list. The form renderer handles all field types.
- The portal checks if a client has already submitted via:
```ts
supabase.from('form_responses')
  .select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })
  .eq('form_id', form.id)
  .eq('client_id', clientId)
```
- Responses are submitted via `/api/forms/submit` (API route, bypasses RLS with service role)

### Existing parsing/display of response data
- **Admin side**: `lib/formService.ts` `exportResponsesToCSV()` parses `response_data` by iterating fields
- **No existing UI display** of individual response field values — the admin forms page likely shows responses in a table but reading the response field-by-field requires field lookup
- **Portal side**: No rendering of form response data at all — the portal only checks if an onboarding response *exists* (the count query), it does not display the answers

---

## 2. Client Data for Resource Vault

### `clients` table (live DB — full column list)

| Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | uuid | NO | |
| `email` | varchar | NO | Used for portal auth |
| `is_active` | boolean | NO | default true |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | NO | |
| `updated_at` | timestamptz | NO | |
| `name` | varchar | NO | |
| `location` | varchar | YES | |
| `source` | varchar | YES | |
| `onboarding_call_held` | boolean | YES | |
| `onboarding_call_date` | date | YES | |
| `is_whitelisted` | boolean | YES | |
| `whitelist_partner_id` | uuid | YES | FK to partners |
| `archived_at` | timestamptz | YES | soft delete |
| `slug` | varchar | YES | used for portal URL |
| `logo_url` | text | YES | used in portal header |
| `approved_domains` | ARRAY | YES | email domain whitelist |

**Conclusion for Resource Vault: There are NO resource-related fields on `clients`** — no `telegram_group`, no `shared_drive_url`, no `notion_url`, no `drive_link`, nothing of the sort. These would all need new DB columns.

### What the clients page shows for each client
The clients page (`app/clients/page.tsx`) shows `logo_url` and uses `approved_domains` for portal access control. It has a "Share Portal" dialog that shows:
- The portal URL: `/public/portal/{slug or id}`
- A hardcoded onboarding form link (slug `holo-hive-onboarding`)
- Additional shareable form links from `shareExtraForms` state

No resource links (Telegram, Drive, Notion, etc.) are rendered anywhere in the clients page.

---

## 3. `client_context` Table

### Live schema

| Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | uuid | NO | |
| `client_id` | uuid | NO | FK to clients |
| `engagement_type` | text | YES | |
| `scope` | text | YES | |
| `start_date` | date | YES | |
| `milestones` | text | YES | |
| `client_contacts` | text | YES | |
| `holohive_contacts` | text | YES | |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | YES | |
| `updated_at` | timestamptz | YES | |

**No resource-related fields here either.** This table is purely relationship/engagement metadata (scope, milestones, contacts). The portal fetches it with `select('id, engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts')`.

---

## Summary: What Exists vs. What Needs New DB Fields

**Already exists (can be used directly):**
- `form_responses.response_data` (JSONB, keyed by field UUID) — available, but displaying it requires a field-lookup join
- `form_responses.client_id` — links responses to clients
- `clients.logo_url` — displayed in portal
- `clients.slug` — used for portal URL
- `clients.approved_domains` — portal access control

**Does NOT exist — needs new DB columns for Resource Vault:**
- `telegram_group` / `telegram_url` — not on `clients` or `client_context`
- `shared_drive_url` / `google_drive_url` — does not exist
- Any `notion_url`, `resource_links`, or similar — does not exist

The cleanest approach would be to either add columns directly to the `clients` table (e.g., `telegram_group_url text`, `shared_drive_url text`, `notion_url text`) or create a new `client_resources` table if you want a more flexible key-value structure for multiple resource links.

```text
    26→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_report_publicly BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    27→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS report_share_link TEXT;
    28→
    29→-- Create indexes for better performance
    30→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_reports_campaign_id ON public.campaign_reports(campaign_id);
    31→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_campaign_id ON public.campaign_report_files(campaign_id);
    32→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_is_public ON public.campaign_report_files(is_public);
    33→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_display_order ON public.campaign_report_files(display_order);
    34→
    35→-- Enable Row Level Security
    36→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_reports ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    37→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_report_files ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    38→
    39→-- RLS Policies for campaign_reports
    40→CREATE POLICY "Users can view reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    41→    FOR SELECT USING (
    42→        EXISTS (
    43→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    44→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    45→        )
    46→    );
    47→
    48→CREATE POLICY "Users can insert reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    49→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    50→        EXISTS (
    51→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    52→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    53→        )
    54→    );
    55→
    56→CREATE POLICY "Users can update reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    57→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    58→        EXISTS (
    59→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    60→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    61→        )
    62→    );
    63→
    64→-- RLS Policies for campaign_report_files
    65→CREATE POLICY "Users can view files for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_report_files
    66→    FOR SELECT USING (
    67→        EXISTS (
    68→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    69→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    70→        )
    71→        OR is_public = true
    72→    );
    73→
    74→CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    75→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    76→        auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
    77→        AND EXISTS (
    78→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    79→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    80→        )
    81→    );
    82→
    83→CREATE POLICY "Users can update their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    84→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    85→        EXISTS (
    86→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    87→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    88→        )
    89→    );
    90→
    91→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    92→    FOR DELETE USING (
    93→        EXISTS (
    94→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    95→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    96→        )
    97→    );
    98→
    99→-- Create updated_at trigger for campaign_reports
   100→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at()
   101→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   102→BEGIN
   103→    NEW.updated_at = timezone('utc'::text, now());
   104→    RETURN NEW;
   105→END;
   106→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   107→
   108→CREATE TRIGGER update_campaign_reports_updated_at
   109→    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.campaign_reports
   110→    FOR EACH ROW
   111→    EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at();
   112→
   113→-- Grant permissions
   114→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_reports TO authenticated;
   115→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_report_files TO authenticated;
   116→GRANT SELECT ON public.campaign_report_files TO anon;
   117→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/025_campaigns_rls_policies.sql`

```text
     1→-- Campaigns Row Level Security Policies
     2→-- Applied via Supabase MCP migrations:
     3→-- - add_created_by_to_campaigns
     4→-- - campaigns_rls_access_control
     5→--
     6→-- Access Control Rules:
     7→-- ============================================
     8→-- SELECT:
     9→--   - Admins: can view ALL campaigns
    10→--   - Members: can view campaigns where created_by = user OR manager = user
    11→--
    12→-- INSERT:
    13→--   - All authenticated users can create campaigns
    14→--   - created_by is automatically set to current user in campaignService.ts
    15→--
    16→-- UPDATE:
    17→--   - Admins: can update ALL campaigns
    18→--   - Members: can update campaigns where created_by = user OR manager = user
    19→--
    20→-- DELETE:
    21→--   - Admins: can delete ALL campaigns
    22→--   - Members: can ONLY delete campaigns they created (created_by = user)
    23→--   - Note: Members CANNOT delete campaigns they only manage (but didn't create)
    24→-- ============================================
    25→
    26→-- The following SQL was applied to add the created_by column:
    27→-- ALTER TABLE campaigns ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS created_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id);
    28→-- UPDATE campaigns SET created_by = manager WHERE created_by IS NULL AND manager IS NOT NULL;
    29→-- CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaigns_created_by ON campaigns(created_by);
    30→-- CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaigns_manager ON campaigns(manager);
    31→
    32→-- The following RLS policies were created:
    33→--
    34→-- SELECT POLICIES:
    35→-- "Admins can view all campaigns" - FOR SELECT using users.role = 'admin'
    36→-- "Members can view own or managed campaigns" - FOR SELECT using created_by = auth.uid() OR manager = auth.uid()
    37→-- "public_campaigns_anon_select" - FOR SELECT (anonymous access for public reports)
    38→--
    39→-- INSERT POLICIES:
    40→-- "Users can create campaigns" - FOR INSERT with check true (all authenticated)
    41→--
    42→-- UPDATE POLICIES:
    43→-- "Admins can update all campaigns" - FOR UPDATE using users.role = 'admin'
    44→-- "Members can update own or managed campaigns" - FOR UPDATE using created_by = auth.uid() OR manager = auth.uid()
    45→--
    46→-- DELETE POLICIES:
    47→-- "Admins can delete all campaigns" - FOR DELETE using users.role = 'admin'
    48→-- "Members can delete own campaigns" - FOR DELETE using created_by = auth.uid()
    49→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./supabase_migration_multiple_contents.sql`

```text
     1→-- Migration: Change payments.content_id to support multiple content IDs
     2→-- This migration converts the content_id column from a single UUID to an array of UUIDs
     3→
     4→-- Step 1: Add a new temporary column to store the array of content IDs
     5→ALTER TABLE payments
     6→ADD COLUMN content_ids uuid[];
     7→
     8→-- Step 2: Migrate existing data from content_id to content_ids
     9→-- Convert existing single content_id values to single-item arrays
    10→UPDATE payments
    11→SET content_ids = ARRAY[content_id]
    12→WHERE content_id IS NOT NULL;
    13→
    14→-- Step 3: Drop the old content_id column
    15→ALTER TABLE payments
    16→DROP COLUMN content_id;
    17→
    18→-- Step 4: Rename content_ids to content_id
    19→ALTER TABLE payments
    20→RENAME COLUMN content_ids TO content_id;
    21→
    22→-- Step 5: Add comment to the column
    23→COMMENT ON COLUMN payments.content_id IS 'Array of content IDs linked to this payment';
    24→
    25→-- Optional: Create an index on the array column for better query performance
    26→CREATE INDEX idx_payments_content_id_gin ON payments USING GIN (content_id);
    27→
    28→-- Note: If you have any foreign key constraints on content_id, you'll need to handle those separately
    29→-- You may also need to update any RLS (Row Level Security) policies that reference content_id
    30→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
==> /Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts <==
import { supabase } from './supabase';
import { Database } from './database.types';
import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';

type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];

export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
  member_count?: number;
  campaign_count?: number;
  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
}

export class ClientService {
  /**
   * Get clients based on user role
   * - Admins: See all clients
   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
   */
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-11 18:38:06Z)**_

I now have a comprehensive picture of the entire data structure. Here is the full findings report:

---

## Complete Data Structure Report: KOL Campaign Data for Client Portal

Primary source: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/database.types.ts`
Service layer: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/campaignKolService.ts`, `campaignService.ts`
Public page query: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/public/campaigns/[id]/page.tsx`

---

### 1. `campaign_kols` Table

**Columns (from `database.types.ts` Row type):**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` (UUID) | Primary key |
| `campaign_id` | `string` (UUID) | FK → `campaigns.id` |
| `master_kol_id` | `string` (UUID) | FK → `master_kols.id` |
| `hh_status` | `string \| null` | Internal status. Enum values: `'Curated'`, `'Contacted'`, `'Interested'`, `'Onboarded'`, `'Concluded'` |
| `client_status` | `string \| null` | Client-facing status. Enum values: `'Rejected'`, `'Preferred'` |
| `allocated_budget` | `number \| null` | Budget assigned to this KOL |
| `budget_type` | `string \| null` | Enum values: `'Token'`, `'Fiat'`, `'WL'` |
| `paid` | `number \| null` | Amount actually paid |
| `wallet` | `string \| null` | Payment wallet address |
| `notes` | `string \| null` | Internal notes |
| `created_at` | `string \| null` | Timestamp |
| `updated_at` | `string \| null` | Timestamp |

**Additional column NOT in `database.types.ts` but used in code:**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hidden` | `boolean \| null` | Controls visibility on the public portal. Filtered with `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')` in the public page. The admin UI has an "Active" vs "Hidden" tab that toggles this. |

**Relationships:**
- `campaign_kols.campaign_id` → `campaigns.id` (many-to-one)
- `campaign_kols.master_kol_id` → `master_kols.id` (many-to-one)
- `contents.campaign_kols_id` → `campaign_kols.id` (one-to-many contents per KOL assignment)
- `payments.campaign_kol_id` → `campaign_kols.id`

**Standard query used in the public page (`fetchData` in the public campaign page):**
```typescript
supabasePublic
  .from('campaign_kols')
  .select(`id, hh_status, client_status, allocated_budget, budget_type, notes,
           master_kol:master_kols(id, name, link, followers, platform, region, content_type, creator_type)`)
  .eq('campaign_id', actualCampaignId)
  .or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
```

**Full query used in admin service (`campaignKolService.ts`):**
```typescript
supabase
  .from('campaign_kols')
  .select(`*, master_kol:master_kols(*)`)
  .eq('campaign_id', campaignId)
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
```

---

### 2. `contents` Table

**All columns:**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` (UUID) | Primary key |
| `campaign_id` | `string` (UUID) | FK → `campaigns.id` |
| `campaign_kols_id` | `string` (UUID) | FK → `campaign_kols.id` |
| `platform` | `string \| null` | e.g. `'X'`, `'Telegram'`, `'YouTube'`, `'TikTok'`, `'Facebook'` |
| `type` | `string \| null` | e.g. `'Post'`, `'Video'`, `'Article'`, `'AMA'`, `'Ambassadorship'`, `'Alpha'`, `'QRT'`, `'Thread'`, `'Spaces'`, `'Newsletter'` |
| `status` | `string \| null` | Content lifecycle status |
| `activation_date` | `string \| null` | Date content went live |
| `content_link` | `string \| null` | Live URL to the published content |
| `impressions` | `number \| null` | Impression count |
| `likes` | `number \| null` | Like count |
| `retweets` | `number \| null` | Retweet/share count |
| `comments` | `number \| null` | Comment count |
| `bookmarks` | `number \| null` | Bookmark count |
| `created_at` | `string \| null` | Timestamp |
| `updated_at` | `string \| null` | Timestamp |

**Relationships:**
- `contents.campaign_id` → `campaigns.id`
- `contents.campaign_kols_id` → `campaign_kols.id`
- `payments.content_id` → `contents.id` (note: `payments.content_id` was migrated to a `uuid[]` array per `supabase_migration_multiple_contents.sql`)

**Query used in public page:**
```typescript
supabasePublic
  .from('contents')
  .select('*, campaign_kol:campaign_kols(master_kol:master_kols(id, name, link))')
  .eq('campaign_id', actualCampaignId)
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
```

---

### 3. `campaigns` Table and Client Linkage

**All columns (base schema from `database.types.ts`):**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` (UUID) | Primary key |
| `client_id` | `string` (UUID) | FK → `clients.id` — this is the client link |
| `name` | `string` | Campaign name |
| `status` | `string` | `'Planning'`, `'Active'`, `'Paused'`, `'Completed'` |
| `total_budget` | `number` | Total campaign budget |
| `start_date` | `string` | |
| `end_date` | `string` | |
| `description` | `string \| null` | |
| `outline` | `string \| null` | |
| `region` | `string \| null` | e.g. `'Vietnam'`, `'Turkey'`, `'SEA'`, `'Philippines'`, `'Korea'`, `'Global'`, etc. |
| `manager` | `string \| null` | User assigned as manager |
| `budget_type` | `string[] \| null` | Array of budget types |
| `intro_call` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `intro_call_date` | `string \| null` | |
| `proposal_sent` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `nda_signed` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `call_support` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `client_choosing_kols` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `multi_activation` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `share_report_publicly` | `boolean \| null` | Added via migration `20250131_create_campaign_reports.sql` |
| `report_share_link` | `string \| null` | Added via same migration |
| `created_at` | `string` | |
| `updated_at` | `string` | |

**Additional columns used in code but not in `database.types.ts` (added via Supabase MCP migrations, types file out of date):**

| Column | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `slug` | `string \| null` | URL-friendly slug for sharing; generated via `generateUniqueSlug(name)` on create |
| `approved_emails` | `string[] \| null` | List of emails authorized to view the public campaign portal |
| `approved_domains` | `string[] \| null` | List of email domains authorized for access |
| `archived_at` | `timestamp \| null` | Soft-delete; filtered with `.is('archived_at', null)` in queries |
| `created_by` | `UUID \| null` | References `auth.users(id)`; used for member-level RLS access control |
| `share_creator_type` | `boolean \| null` | Whether to share `creator_type` field on the public portal |
| `share_kol_notes` | `boolean \| null` | Whether to share KOL `notes` on the public portal |

**Client linkage — full chain:**
```
clients.id
  ← campaigns.client_id          (campaigns belong to a client)
    ← campaign_kols.campaign_id  (KOL assignments belong to a campaign)
      ← contents.campaign_id     (content belongs to a campaign)
      ← contents.campaign_kols_id (content also linked directly to KOL assignment)
```

**Campaign → Client join used in service layer:**
```typescript
.select(`
  *,
  clients!campaigns_client_id_fkey(name, email, logo_url),
  campaign_budget_allocations(*)
`)
```

---

### 4. `clients` Table

**Base columns (`database.types.ts`):**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` (UUID) | Primary key |
| `name` | `string` | |
| `email` | `string` | Primary authorized email for portal access |
| `is_active` | `boolean` | |
| `is_whitelisted` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `location` | `string \| null` | |
| `source` | `string \| null` | |
| `onboarding_call_date` | `string \| null` | |
| `onboarding_call_held` | `boolean \| null` | |
| `whitelist_partner_id` | `string \| null` | FK → `partners.id` |
| `created_at` | `string` | |
| `updated_at` | `string` | |

**Additional columns added via migrations (not in `database.types.ts`):**

| Column | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `logo_url` | `TEXT \| null` | Added by `20250121_add_client_logo.sql` — used for client branding in portal |
| `slug` | `VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE \| null` | Added by `20250121_add_client_slug.sql` — for clean portal URLs |
| `approved_domains` | `string[] \| null` | Used in public page for domain-level access control; merged with campaign-level domains |

**Related table — `client_access_members`:**
Controls which internal users (`users.id`) have access to which clients. Columns: `id`, `client_id` (FK → `clients`), `user_id` (FK → `users`), `created_at`.

---

### 5. `master_kols` Table (the KOL Profile)

**All columns:**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` (UUID) | Primary key |
| `name` | `string` | KOL display name |
| `link` | `string \| null` | Profile URL / main social link |
| `platform` | `string[] \| null` | Array, e.g. `['X', 'Telegram']`, `['YouTube']` |
| `followers` | `number \| null` | Follower count |
| `region` | `string \| null` | Geographic region |
| `tier` | `string \| null` | `'Tier S'`, `'Tier 1'`, `'Tier 2'`, `'Tier 3'`, `'Tier 4'` (enforced by constraint per `015_add_tier_s.sql`) |
| `niche` | `string[] \| null` | Array of niche categories |
| `content_type` | `string[] \| null` | Content format array |
| `creator_type` | `string[] \| null` | e.g. `'Native (Meme/Culture)'`, `'Educator'`, `'Skeptic'`, `'Drama-Forward'`, `'Bridge Builder'`, `'Visionary'`, `'Onboarder'`, `'General'`, `'Gaming'`, `'Crypto'`, etc. |
| `deliverables` | `string[] \| null` | Array of deliverable types |
| `description` | `string \| null` | Bio / notes |
| `pricing` | `string \| null` | Pricing info (free text) |
| `rating` | `number \| null` | Internal rating score |
| `community` | `boolean \| null` | Has community (group/channel) |
| `group_chat` | `boolean \| null` | Has a group chat |
| `created_at` | `string \| null` | |
| `updated_at` | `string \| null` | |

**Additional column added via migration:**

| Column | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `in_house` | `TEXT \| null` | Added by `014_add_in_house_column.sql` — in-house status |

Note: `archived_at` is used in code queries (`.is('archived_at', null)`) to filter active KOLs, but is not in `database.types.ts`. It was added to the live DB via a Supabase MCP migration.

---

### 6. Supplementary Tables Relevant to Client Portal

**`campaign_budget_allocations`** — per-region budget breakdown for a campaign:
- `id`, `campaign_id` (FK), `region` (string), `allocated_budget` (number), `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`campaign_updates`** — text updates/announcements for a campaign:
- `id`, `campaign_id` (FK), `update_text` (string), `created_at`, `updated_at`

**`campaign_reports` + `campaign_report_files`** — report files uploaded for a campaign:
- `campaign_report_files`: `id`, `campaign_id`, `file_name`, `file_url`, `file_type`, `file_size`, `is_public` (boolean), `uploaded_by`, `display_order`, `created_at`
- These are filtered by `is_public = true` for public/client access

**`payments`** — payment records:
- `id`, `campaign_id`, `campaign_kol_id` (FK), `content_id` (now `uuid[]` after migration), `amount`, `payment_method`, `payment_date`, `transaction_id`, `notes`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

---

### Key Design Notes for the Client Portal

1. **Access control** on the public page is purely email-based (no Supabase Auth). Users enter an email; it is matched against `clients.email`, `campaigns.approved_emails`, or email domains from `campaigns.approved_domains` / `clients.approved_domains`.

2. **KOL visibility**: `campaign_kols.hidden = true` suppresses a KOL from the public view. The public page query explicitly filters `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')`.

3. **Selective data sharing**: `campaigns.share_creator_type` and `campaigns.share_kol_notes` are boolean flags that control whether `creator_type` and `notes` fields are shown in the portal view.

4. **Content metrics available per KOL content item**: impressions, likes, retweets, comments, bookmarks — all on the `contents` table, linked via `campaign_kols_id`.

5. **The `payments.content_id` column was migrated from a single UUID to a `uuid[]` array** — if querying payment data in a portal, use the GIN index and array operators.

## Implementation Plan: Three Portal Features

Here is the complete, detailed implementation plan for all three features. I'll address each feature independently, then cover cross-cutting concerns (migration, RLS, admin side changes) at the end.

---

### Codebase Summary (What I Found)

**Portal page** (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`): 1,454 lines, `'use client'`, uses `supabasePublic` (anon client), 8 existing data-fetch `async function`s, structured as a single large component. The page renders in order: Header, Welcome, Onboarding Banner, Client Context, Stats Cards, Weekly Status, Campaigns section, Meeting Notes, Decision Log, Footer.

**`client_context` columns confirmed:** `id, client_id, engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts, created_at, updated_at` — no resource columns yet.

**RLS analysis:**
- `campaign_kols`: has `public_campaign_kols_anon_select` policy (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT already works.
- `contents`: has `public_contents_anon_select` policy (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT already works.
- `master_kols`: has `public_master_kols_anon_select` + `Public can view shared master KOLs` (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT works.
- `client_context`: has `Anon users can select client_context` (`qual: true`) — works.
- `form_responses`: has `Clients can check their own responses` (`qual: client_id IS NOT NULL`) — anon CAN read because the policy has no `auth.uid()` check, just requires the column not be null. This works as-is.
- `form_fields`: has `Anyone can view published form fields` policy which requires the form to have `status = 'published'` — the `holo-hive-onboarding` form IS published, so anon can already read fields. No new policy needed.
- `forms`: has `Anyone can view published forms` (`status = 'published'`) — works.

**Onboarding form fields confirmed:** 22 fields across 7 pages. Labels contain HTML (inline styles, bold tags). Need to strip HTML when displaying. The answer-bearing field types are: `text`, `textarea`, `select`. `section` and `description` type fields have no corresponding `response_data` values and must be skipped. Field IDs are the keys in `response_data` JSONB.

**Admin context edit form** is a Dialog in `app/clients/page.tsx` using `handleContextSubmit()` which does update/insert to `client_context`. The `contextForm` state must be extended to include resource fields.

---

### Feature 1: KOL Roster

#### Data Fetch Strategy

The Supabase query chain is: `campaigns` (for this `client_id`) → `campaign_kols` (for each `campaign_id`, filter `hidden != true`) → join to `master_kols` (via `master_kol_id`) → join to `contents` (via `campaign_kols_id`).

The most efficient single query uses Supabase's nested select with an explicit or-filter for the `hidden` column:

```typescript
async function fetchKolRoster() {
  if (!clientId) return;
  try {
    // Step 1: Get all campaigns for this client
    const { data: campaignsData } = await supabasePublic
      .from('campaigns')
      .select('id, name, status')
      .eq('client_id', clientId)
      .is('archived_at', null)
      .order('start_date', { ascending: false });

    if (!campaignsData?.length) {
      setKolRoster([]);
      return;
    }

    const campaignIds = campaignsData.map(c => c.id);

    // Step 2: Fetch campaign_kols with nested master_kols and contents
    const { data: kolsData } = await supabasePublic
      .from('campaign_kols')
      .select(`
        id,
        campaign_id,
        hh_status,
        client_status,
        allocated_budget,
        hidden,
        master_kols (
          id,
          name,
          link,
          platform,
          followers,
          tier,
          niche,
          region,
          content_type,
          creator_type
        ),
        contents (
          id,
          platform,
          type,
          status,
          content_link,
          activation_date,
          impressions,
          likes,
          retweets,
          comments,
          bookmarks
        )
      `)
      .in('campaign_id', campaignIds)
      .or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false');

    // Step 3: Group by campaign
    const rosterByCampaign = campaignsData.map(campaign => ({
      campaign,
      kols: (kolsData || []).filter(k => k.campaign_id === campaign.id)
    })).filter(g => g.kols.length > 0);

    setKolRoster(rosterByCampaign);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error fetching KOL roster:', err);
  }
}
```

#### New Types to Add

```typescript
type KolRosterEntry = {
  id: string;                    // campaign_kols.id
  campaign_id: string;
  hh_status: string | null;
  client_status: string | null;
  allocated_budget: number | null;
  master_kols: {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    link: string | null;
    platform: string[] | null;
    followers: number | null;
    tier: string | null;
    niche: string[] | null;
    region: string | null;
    content_type: string[] | null;
    creator_type: string[] | null;
  } | null;
  contents: {
    id: string;
    platform: string | null;
    type: string | null;
    status: string | null;
    content_link: string | null;
    activation_date: string | null;
    impressions: number | null;
    likes: number | null;
    retweets: number | null;
    comments: number | null;
    bookmarks: number | null;
  }[];
};

type KolRosterGroup = {
  campaign: { id: string; name: string; status: string };
  kols: KolRosterEntry[];
};
```

#### Status Mapping

```typescript

  'Curated': 'Shortlisted',
  'Contacted': 'Pitching',
  'Interested': 'Negotiating',
  'Onboarded': 'Content Creation',
  'Concluded': 'Completed',
};
```

Match the existing portal color system:

- Pitching → yellow-100/yellow-800
- Negotiating → orange-100/orange-800
- Content Creation → purple-100/purple-800
- Completed → gray-100/gray-800

```typescript

const [kolRosterLoading, setKolRosterLoading] = useState(false);
```

#### Call Site

Add `fetchKolRoster()` to the existing `useEffect` that fires when `isAuthenticated && clientId`.

#### Render Location

After the Campaigns `</Card>` closing tag and before Meeting Notes. The section is always rendered (if `kolRoster.length > 0`).

#### UI Structure

```
<Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mt-8">
  <CardHeader>
    <Users icon> "KOL Roster" <span>({total count})</span>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    {kolRoster.map(group => (
      <div key={group.campaign.id}>
        {/* Campaign header */}
        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-3 mt-6 first:mt-0">
          <Badge status-color>{group.campaign.status}</Badge>
          <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900">{group.campaign.name}</h3>
          <span className="text-sm text-gray-400">({group.kols.length} KOLs)</span>
        </div>
        {/* Table */}
        <div className="overflow-x-auto">
          <table className="w-full text-sm">
            <thead>
              <tr className="text-xs text-gray-500 uppercase border-b">
                <th>KOL</th><th>Platform</th><th>Tier</th>
                <th>Status</th><th>Content</th><th>Impressions</th><th>Engagement</th>
              </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
              {group.kols.map(kol => {
                const totalImpressions = kol.contents.reduce(...)
                const totalEngagement = kol.contents.reduce(...)
                const liveLinks = kol.contents.filter(c => c.content_link)
                return (
                  <tr key={kol.id} className="border-b last:border-0 hover:bg-gray-50">
                    <td>
                      {kol.master_kols?.link
                        ? <a href={kol.master_kols.link} target="_blank">{kol.master_kols?.name}<ExternalLink/></a>
                        : kol.master_kols?.name}
                    </td>
                    <td>{platforms as badges}</td>
                    <td><Badge>{kol.master_kols?.tier}</Badge></td>
                    <td><Badge>{HH_STATUS_LABELS[kol.hh_status] || kol.hh_status}</Badge></td>
                    <td>
                      {liveLinks.length > 0
                        ? liveLinks.map(c => <a href={c.content_link} target="_blank"><ExternalLink/></a>)
                        : <span className="text-gray-400">{kol.contents.length > 0 ? kol.contents.length + ' pieces' : '—'}</span>}
                    </td>
                    <td>{formatNumber(totalImpressions) || '—'}</td>
                    <td>{formatNumber(totalEngagement) || '—'}</td>
                  </tr>
                )
              })}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>
      </div>
    ))}
  </CardContent>
</Card>
```

#### New Icons Needed

### Feature 2: Campaign DNA

#### Data Fetch Strategy

Three sequential fetches (form fields can be fetched in parallel with the response):

```typescript
async function fetchCampaignDNA() {
  if (!clientId) return;
  try {
    // 1. Get the onboarding form (already fetched in checkOnboardingStatus, reuse form id)
    // The form id '911f79b1-...' can be looked up by slug if not cached

    // 2. Fetch form fields AND form response in parallel
    const [fieldsResult, responseResult] = await Promise.all([
      supabasePublic
        .from('form_fields')
        .select('id, label, field_type, display_order, page_number')
        .eq('form_id', onboardingFormId)
        .not('field_type', 'in', '("section","description")')
        .order('page_number')
        .order('display_order'),
      supabasePublic
        .from('form_responses')
        .select('response_data, submitted_at')
        .eq('form_id', onboardingFormId)
        .eq('client_id', clientId)
        .order('submitted_at', { ascending: false })
        .limit(1)
        .single()
    ]);

    if (!responseResult.data) return; // no submission yet

    setDnaFields(fieldsResult.data || []);
    setDnaResponse(responseResult.data);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error fetching campaign DNA:', err);
  }
}
```

**Key note on timing:** `checkOnboardingStatus()` already fetches the form id. The easiest approach is to store the form id in state (not just the slug), then `fetchCampaignDNA()` depends on that id. Alternatively, accept the redundant form lookup inside `fetchCampaignDNA`.

#### HTML Label Stripping

Form field labels contain inline HTML (font tags, span style attributes). A small utility is needed:

```typescript
const stripHtml = (html: string): string => {
  // Use DOM parser in client context, or regex for simple case
  return html.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '').replace(/&amp;/g, '&').replace(/&quot;/g, '"').trim();
};

// Extract the clean question label: "1. Key Objectives: What are your main goals?"
// (labels contain number prefix and bold formatting)
```

#### Page-to-Section Grouping

The 7 form pages map to readable section names:
- Page 1: (intro/section only, no answer fields — already filtered out by `not field_type in (section, description)`)
- Page 2: "Project Vision" (fields: Key Objectives, Success Metrics, Elevator Pitch)
- Page 3: "Team & Timeline" (fields: Team Roles, Decision-Making, Milestones)
- Page 4: "Details & Assets" (fields: Tokenomics, Brand Assets, Materials, Project Links)
- Page 5: "Rewards" (field: Incentive Systems)
- Page 6: "Compliance" (field: Regulatory Compliance)
- Page 7: "Final Check" (field: Anything else)

Group by `page_number` when rendering.

#### New Types

```typescript
type DnaField = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  field_type: string;
  display_order: number;
  page_number: number;
};

type DnaResponse = {
  response_data: Record<string, any>;
  submitted_at: string | null;
};
```

#### New State

```typescript
const [dnaFields, setDnaFields] = useState<DnaField[]>([]);
const [dnaResponse, setDnaResponse] = useState<DnaResponse | null>(null);
const [onboardingFormId, setOnboardingFormId] = useState<string | null>(null);
```

Update `checkOnboardingStatus()` to also `setOnboardingFormId(form.id)`.

#### Render Location

After the Engagement Overview (Client Context) `</Card>` and before Stats Cards. Condition: `hasOnboardingResponse === true && dnaResponse !== null`.

#### UI Structure

```
<Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mb-10">
  <CardHeader>
    <FileText icon> "Campaign Brief"
    <span className="ml-auto text-sm text-gray-400">
      Submitted {formatDate(dnaResponse.submitted_at)}
    </span>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    {/* Group fields by page_number */}
    {groupedPages.map(page => {
      const answeredFields = page.fields.filter(f => dnaResponse.response_data[f.id]);
      if (!answeredFields.length) return null;
      return (
        <div key={page.pageNumber} className="mb-6 last:mb-0">
          <h4 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-400 uppercase tracking-wider mb-3">
            {PAGE_SECTION_NAMES[page.pageNumber] || `Section ${page.pageNumber}`}
          </h4>
          <div className="space-y-4">
            {answeredFields.map(field => (
              <div key={field.id}>
                <p className="text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 mb-1">
                  {stripHtml(field.label)}
                </p>
                <p className="text-sm text-gray-800 whitespace-pre-wrap leading-relaxed">
                  {String(dnaResponse.response_data[field.id])}
                </p>
              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
          {/* Divider between sections */}
        </div>
      );
    })}
  </CardContent>
</Card>
```

#### RLS Confirmation

- `forms`: `Anyone can view published forms` — works (holo-hive-onboarding is published).
- `form_fields`: `Anyone can view published form fields` — works (linked form is published).
- `form_responses`: `Clients can check their own responses` with `qual: client_id IS NOT NULL` — this policy applies to ALL roles including anon, and requires only that `client_id` column is not null. Since the portal correctly passes `.eq('client_id', clientId)`, this will match rows where `client_id = clientId`. This works as-is with no new policy needed.

### Feature 3: Resource Vault

#### Database Migration

Add three individual columns to `client_context` (your recommended approach):

```sql
-- Migration: add_resource_columns_to_client_context
ALTER TABLE client_context
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS telegram_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS shared_drive_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gtm_sync_url TEXT;

COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.telegram_url IS 'Telegram group invite link for this client';
COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.shared_drive_url IS 'Shared Google Drive or similar link';
COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.gtm_sync_url IS 'GTM sync / Notion / campaign tracker URL';
```

File name: `supabase/migrations/20260312_add_resource_columns_to_client_context.sql`

No new RLS policy needed — `client_context` already has `Anon users can select client_context` (`qual: true`).

#### Portal Side

**New state:**

The `ClientContext` type already drives the portal display. Since `fetchClientContext()` selects specific columns, add the three new columns to both the TypeScript type and the `.select()` call:

```typescript
};
```

```typescript
.select('id, engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts, telegram_url, shared_drive_url, gtm_sync_url')
```

No new fetch function — the resources come from the same `client_context` row.

---

### Implementation Sequence

Follow this order to avoid blocking issues:

**Step 1 — Database migration** (must be first):
Run the SQL migration to add the three columns to `client_context`. Everything else depends on this for Feature 3.

**Step 2 — Feature 3: Resource Vault** (simplest, good warm-up):
- Update `ClientContext` type in both files.
- Update `contextForm` state and `handleContextSubmit` in `clients/page.tsx`.
- Add fields to the Context Modal UI in `clients/page.tsx`.
- Update `fetchClientContext()` select in portal page.
- Add Resources card to portal JSX.

**Step 3 — Feature 2: Campaign DNA** (medium complexity):
- Add `onboardingFormId` state, update `checkOnboardingStatus` to store it.
- Add `fetchCampaignDNA()` function.
- Add `dnaFields`, `dnaResponse` state.
- Add `stripHtml` utility function.
- Add page-section name map.
- Add Campaign Brief card to portal JSX, placed after the Engagement Overview card.

**Step 4 — Feature 1: KOL Roster** (most complex):
- Add `KolRosterEntry`, `KolRosterGroup` types.
- Add `kolRoster`, `kolRosterLoading` state.
- Add `fetchKolRoster()` function.
- Add `HH_STATUS_LABELS` map and `getKolStatusBadge()` helper.
- Add `Link2` to lucide-react imports.
- Add KOL Roster card to portal JSX after the Campaigns card.

---

### Edge Cases and Gotchas

**Feature 1 — KOL Roster:**
- The `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')` filter must be applied as a PostgREST OR filter: `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')` at the `campaign_kols` query level.
- If a client has many campaigns (e.g., 20+) with many KOLs each, the `campaign_ids` array in `.in('campaign_id', campaignIds)` could be large. This is fine for typical client scales but worth noting.
- `master_kols` relation: Supabase nested select using `master_kols (...)` will return the object directly (not an array) since `master_kol_id` is a single FK. TypeScript type should be `master_kols: { ... } | null` not an array.
- `contents` relation: returns an array via `campaign_kols_id` FK. Already confirmed by column schema.
- KOLs with `client_status = 'Rejected'` are not explicitly hidden but may be confusing to clients. Since you haven't specified filtering them, leave them visible — but if needed, add `.neq('client_status', 'Rejected')` to the query.

**Feature 2 — Campaign DNA:**
- Form field labels are HTML strings — `stripHtml` is essential. The regex approach `html.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')` is safe here since there's no untrusted content, just the admin's own HTML styling.
- Some fields may have empty/null values in `response_data`. Filter with `if (!dnaResponse.response_data[field.id]) continue`.
- The `response_data` keys are field UUIDs. The map from field id → label is built by indexing `dnaFields` by `id`.
- `select` type fields (e.g., Decision-Making, Incentive Systems) may store their answer as a string value — render it as plain text.
- The `fetchCampaignDNA()` call depends on `onboardingFormId` being set. It should be called inside `checkOnboardingStatus` after form ID is known, or triggered via a `useEffect` dependency on `onboardingFormId`.

**Feature 3 — Resource Vault:**
- All three new columns are nullable. The portal card must only render when at least one has a value — the condition handles this.
- URL validation is not needed on display; if an admin enters a bad URL it simply opens a broken link.
- The `refreshClientContexts()` function in `clients/page.tsx` uses `select('*')` so it will automatically include new columns after migration.

---

### Files to Modify Summary

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` | Add types, state, 3 fetch functions, 3 new card sections |
| `app/clients/page.tsx` | Extend `ClientContext` type, `contextForm` state, `handleContextSubmit`, `openContextModal`, Context Modal UI |
| New migration SQL file | `ALTER TABLE client_context ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS telegram_url TEXT, shared_drive_url TEXT, gtm_sync_url TEXT` |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` - Core file receiving all 3 feature additions; contains all existing fetch patterns, state management, and JSX layout to follow
- `app/clients/page.tsx` - Admin side for Resource Vault; contains the `ClientContext` type definition and the Context Modal form that needs new fields (around lines 49-61 for type, lines 130 and 665-688 for form logic, lines 2647-2724 for modal UI)
- `supabase/migrations/20260312_add_resource_columns_to_client_context.sql` - New migration file (following the pattern of `20250121_add_client_logo.sql`) to add the three resource URL columns to `client_context` before any code changes are deployed
- `lib/clientService.ts` - Reference for understanding how the authenticated Supabase client patterns differ from the portal's `supabasePublic` anon client; no modifications needed but useful context for ensuring queries are issued correctly against the anon client

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-11 18:38:42Z)**_

I need to plan the implementation of 3 features for a client portal page at `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`. This is a public-facing page that uses Supabase anon client (no auth, email-gated).

## Feature 1: KOL Roster
Show an interactive table of KOLs across all campaigns for this client. Data already exists.

**Data chain:** `clients.id` → `campaigns.client_id` → `campaign_kols.campaign_id` → `master_kols` (via `master_kol_id`)
Also: `contents` table linked via `campaign_kols_id` for status/live links.

**Available fields on `master_kols`:** name, link, platform (string[]), followers, region, tier (Tier S/1/2/3/4), niche (string[]), content_type (string[]), creator_type (string[])
**Available fields on `campaign_kols`:** hh_status (Curated/Contacted/Interested/Onboarded/Concluded), client_status (Rejected/Preferred), allocated_budget, hidden (boolean)
**Available fields on `contents`:** platform, type, status, content_link, activation_date, impressions, likes, retweets, comments, bookmarks

**Important:** campaign_kols with `hidden=true` must be filtered out (`.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')`).

**What to show per KOL row:**
- KOL Name (linked to their profile via `master_kols.link`)
- Platform(s) from master_kols
- Tier
- Status — map `hh_status` to client-friendly labels (e.g., Curated→"Shortlisted", Contacted→"Pitching", Interested→"Negotiating", Onboarded→"Content Creation", Concluded→"Completed")
- Content count and live link(s) from contents table
- Basic metrics (impressions, engagement) aggregated from contents

**Group by campaign** — show campaign name as a header, then KOLs under it.

## Feature 2: Campaign DNA (Parsed Form Summary)
When the client has submitted the onboarding form, parse their `form_responses.response_data` (JSONB keyed by field UUIDs) and display it as a clean "Campaign Snapshot" card.

**Key challenge:** `response_data` is keyed by field UUIDs, not labels. To display, we need to also fetch `form_fields` for the onboarding form to get the labels.

**Approach:**
1. Fetch the onboarding form by slug `holo-hive-onboarding`
2. Fetch its fields from `form_fields` (id, label, field_type, display_order)
3. Fetch the form_response for this client
4. Map field IDs to labels and render as a clean summary card

**Display:** A card titled "Campaign Brief" or "Campaign DNA" showing field labels and their answers in a clean layout. Group into a readable format (not raw Q&A dump).

**RLS note:** The portal uses anon Supabase client. There's already a SELECT policy on `form_responses` for anon where `client_id IS NOT NULL`. We'll also need to ensure `form_fields` is readable by anon (check/add policy).

## Feature 3: Resource Vault
Persistent links section at the bottom of the portal — Telegram group, shared drive, campaign tracker, etc.

**Problem:** No resource fields exist on `clients` or `client_context` tables. Need new DB columns.

**Simplest approach:** Add a `resources` JSONB column to `client_context` table (which already has per-client data). This avoids multiple nullable columns and is flexible.

Structure: `{ telegram_group?: string, shared_drive?: string, notion_url?: string, custom?: { label: string, url: string }[] }`

Alternatively, add individual columns to `client_context`: `telegram_url text`, `shared_drive_url text`, `gtm_sync_url text`.

**I recommend individual columns** because:
- Easier to manage in admin UI
- The `client_context` table already has this client's engagement data and is fetched by the portal
- Only 3-4 columns needed

**Admin side:** Need to add these fields to wherever client_context is edited (likely the clients detail page).

**Portal side:** Show a "Resources" card with icons and links. Only show links that have values.

## Current portal structure
The portal already has these sections in order:
1. Header (Holo Hive logo + client name)
2. Welcome text
3. Onboarding Banner (if form not submitted)
4. Engagement Overview (from client_context)
5. Stats Cards (campaign counts)
6. Weekly Status ("What's Active Now")
7. Campaigns list (with search/tabs)
8. Meeting Notes

## Placement of new features
- **Campaign DNA** → After Engagement Overview (section 4), before Stats Cards. Only shows if onboarding form has been submitted.
- **KOL Roster** → After Campaigns list (section 7), before Meeting Notes. Or as a new tab within campaigns.
- **Resource Vault** → At the very bottom, after Meeting Notes.

## Files to modify
- `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` — main portal page, add all 3 features
- `lib/clientService.ts` or wherever client_context is managed — add resource fields to admin editing (but actually check where client_context is edited)
- Supabase migrations — add resource columns to client_context, add RLS policy for form_fields if needed

Please design the detailed implementation plan considering all of this.

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
     6→type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
     9→  member_count?: number;
    10→  campaign_count?: number;
    11→  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class ClientService {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Get clients based on user role
    17→   * - Admins: See all clients
    18→   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
    19→   */
    20→  static async getClientsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string): Promise<ClientWithAccess[]> {
    21→    try {
    22→      if (userRole === 'admin' || userRole === 'super_admin') {
    23→        // Admins and super admins can see all non-archived clients
    24→        const { data: clients, error } = await supabase
    25→          .from('clients')
    26→          .select(`
    27→            *,
    28→            client_access_members(count),
    29→            campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    30→            whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    31→          `)
    32→          .is('archived_at', null)
    33→          .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→        if (error) throw error;
    36→
    37→        return clients?.map(client => ({
    38→          ...client,
    39→          member_count: client.client_access_members?.[0]?.count || 0,
    40→          campaign_count: (client as any).campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    41→          whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    42→        })) || [];
    43→      } else {
    44→        // Members can only see non-archived clients they have access to
    45→        const { data: clientAccess, error } = await supabase
    46→          .from('client_access_members')
    47→          .select(`
    48→            clients!inner(
    49→              id,
    50→              name,
    51→              email,
    52→              location,
    53→              is_active,
    54→              archived_at,
    55→              created_at,
    56→              updated_at,
    57→              logo_url,
    58→              campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    59→              whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    60→            )
    61→          `)
    62→          .eq('user_id', userId);
    63→
    64→        if (error) throw error;
    65→
    66→        return clientAccess?.map(access => {
    67→          const client = (access as any).clients;
    68→          return {
    69→            ...client,
    70→            campaign_count: client.campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    71→            whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    72→          };
    73→        }).filter(c => c && !c.archived_at) || [];
    74→      }
    75→    } catch (error) {
    76→      console.error('Error fetching clients:', error);
    77→      throw error;
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * Get all non-archived clients (admin only)
    83→   */
    84→  static async getAllClients(): Promise<Client[]> {
    85→    try {
    86→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    87→        .from('clients')
    88→        .select('*')
    89→        .is('archived_at', null)
    90→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    91→
    92→      if (error) throw error;
    93→      return data || [];
    94→    } catch (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error fetching all clients:', error);
    96→      throw error;
    97→    }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  /**
   101→   * Create a new client
   102→   */
   103→  static async createClient(
   104→    name: string,
   105→    email: string,
   106→    location?: string,
   107→    source?: string,
   108→    onboarding_call_held?: boolean,
   109→    onboarding_call_date?: string | null,
   110→    is_whitelisted?: boolean,
   111→    whitelist_partner_id?: string | null
   112→  ): Promise<Client> {
   113→    try {
   114→      // Generate a unique slug from the client name
   115→      const slug = generateUniqueSlug(name);
   116→
   117→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→        .from('clients')
   119→        .insert({ name, email, location, source, onboarding_call_held, onboarding_call_date, is_whitelisted, whitelist_partner_id, slug })
   120→        .select()
   121→        .single();
   122→      if (error) throw error;
   123→      return data;
   124→    } catch (error) {
   125→      console.error('Error creating client:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  /**
   131→   * Update client
   132→   */
   133→  static async updateClient(id: string, updates: Partial<Pick<Client, 'name' | 'email' | 'location' | 'is_active' | 'is_whitelisted' | 'whitelist_partner_id' | 'logo_url' | 'approved_domains'>>): Promise<Client> {
   134→    try {
   135→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   136→        .from('clients')
   137→        .update(updates)
   138→        .eq('id', id)
   139→        .select()
   140→        .single();
   141→
   142→      if (error) throw error;
   143→      return data;
   144→    } catch (error) {
   145→      console.error('Error updating client:', error);
   146→      throw error;
   147→    }
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Grant user access to a client
   152→   */
   153→  static async grantClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<ClientAccessMember> {
   154→    try {
   155→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   156→        .from('client_access_members')
   157→        .insert({ client_id: clientId, user_id: userId })
   158→        .select()
   159→        .single();
   160→
   161→      if (error) throw error;
   162→      return data;
   163→    } catch (error) {
   164→      console.error('Error granting client access:', error);
   165→      throw error;
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * Revoke user access from a client
   171→   */
   172→  static async revokeClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
   173→    try {
   174→      const { error } = await supabase
   175→        .from('client_access_members')
   176→        .delete()
   177→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   178→        .eq('user_id', userId);
   179→
   180→      if (error) throw error;
   181→    } catch (error) {
   182→      console.error('Error revoking client access:', error);
   183→      throw error;
   184→    }
   185→  }
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * Get users with access to a specific client
   189→   */
   190→  static async getClientMembers(clientId: string): Promise<any[]> {
   191→    try {
   192→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   193→        .from('client_access_members')
   194→        .select(`
   195→          *,
   196→          users!inner(id, name, email, role)
   197→        `)
   198→        .eq('client_id', clientId);
   199→
   200→      if (error) throw error;
   201→      return data?.map(access => access.users) || [];
   202→    } catch (error) {
   203→      console.error('Error fetching client members:', error);
   204→      throw error;
   205→    }
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  /**
   209→   * Get client by ID or slug (for portal access)
   210→   */
   211→  static async getClientByIdOrSlug(identifier: string): Promise<Client | null> {
   212→    try {
   213→      // Check if identifier is a UUID
   214→      const isUUID = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(identifier);
   215→
   216→      const query = supabase
   217→        .from('clients')
   218→        .select('*')
   219→        .is('archived_at', null);
   220→
   221→      if (isUUID) {
   222→        query.eq('id', identifier);
   223→      } else {
   224→        query.eq('slug', identifier);
   225→      }
   226→
   227→      const { data, error } = await query.single();
   228→
   229→      if (error) {
   230→        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   231→        throw error;
   232→      }
   233→      return data;
   234→    } catch (error) {
   235→      console.error('Error fetching client by ID or slug:', error);
   236→      throw error;
   237→    }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  /**
   241→   * Get client with their campaigns for portal display
   242→   */
   243→  static async getClientWithCampaigns(clientId: string): Promise<{
   244→    client: Client;
   245→    campaigns: any[];
   246→  } | null> {
   247→    try {
   248→      // Get client
   249→      const { data: client, error: clientError } = await supabase
   250→        .from('clients')
   251→        .select('*')
   252→        .eq('id', clientId)
   253→        .is('archived_at', null)
   254→        .single();
   255→
   256→      if (clientError || !client) return null;
   257→
   258→      // Get campaigns for this client with KOL and content counts
   259→      const { data: campaigns, error: campaignsError } = await supabase
   260→        .from('campaigns')
   261→        .select(`
   262→          id,
   263→          name,
   264→          slug,
   265→          status,
   266→          total_budget,
   267→          start_date,
   268→          end_date,
   269→          region,
   270→          description,
   271→          share_report_publicly,
   272→          created_at,
   273→          campaign_kols(count),
   274→          contents(
   275→            impressions,
   276→            likes,
   277→            comments,
   278→            retweets,
   279→            bookmarks
   280→          )
   281→        `)
   282→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   283→        .is('archived_at', null)
   284→        .order('start_date', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→      if (campaignsError) throw campaignsError;
   287→
   288→      // Process campaigns to add aggregated metrics
   289→      const processedCampaigns = campaigns?.map(campaign => {
   290→        const contents = (campaign as any).contents || [];
   291→        const totalImpressions = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) => sum + (c.impressions || 0), 0);
   292→        const totalEngagement = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) =>
   293→          sum + (c.likes || 0) + (c.comments || 0) + (c.retweets || 0) + (c.bookmarks || 0), 0);
   294→
   295→        return {
   296→          ...campaign,
   297→          kol_count: (campaign as any).campaign_kols?.[0]?.count || 0,
   298→          content_count: contents.length,
   299→          total_impressions: totalImpressions,
   300→          total_engagement: totalEngagement,
   301→        };
   302→      }) || [];
   303→
   304→      return { client, campaigns: processedCampaigns };
   305→    } catch (error) {
   306→      console.error('Error fetching client with campaigns:', error);
   307→      throw error;
   308→    }
   309→  }
   310→}
```
</details></tool-use>
