    97→    97→
    98→    98→// Helper to check if a string is a valid UUID
    99→    99→const isUUID = (str: string) => /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(str);
   100→   100→
   101→   101→const formatDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
   102→   102→  if (!dateString) return 'TBD';
   103→   103→  return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' });
   104→   104→};
   105→   105→
   106→   106→const formatCurrency = (amount: number | null) => {
   107→   107→  if (!amount) return '-';
   108→   108→  return new Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', maximumFractionDigits: 0 }).format(amount);
   109→   109→};
   110→   110→
   111→   111→const formatNumber = (num: number) => {
   112→   112→  if (num >= 1000000) return (num / 1000000).toFixed(1) + 'M';
   113→   113→  if (num >= 1000) return (num / 1000).toFixed(1) + 'K';
   114→   114→  return num.toString();
   115→   115→};
   116→   116→
   117→   117→const getStatusBadge = (status: string) => {
   118→   118→  switch (status) {
   119→   119→    case 'Active':
   120→   120→      return 'bg-green-100 text-green-800';
   121→   121→    case 'Planning':
   122→   122→      return 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800';
   123→   123→    case 'Paused':
   124→   124→      return 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800';
   125→   125→    case 'Completed':
   126→   126→      return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-800';
   127→   127→    default:
   128→   128→      return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-800';
   129→   129→  }
   130→   130→};
   131→   131→
   132→   132→export default function ClientPortalPage({ params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
   133→   133→  const router = useRouter();
   134→   134→  const idOrSlug = params.id;
   135→   135→
   136→   136→  // Auth states
   137→   137→  const [clientId, setClientId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   138→   138→  const [client, setClient] = useState<Client | null>(null);
   139→   139→  const [clientEmail, setClientEmail] = useState<string | null>(null);
   140→   140→  const [email, setEmail] = useState('');
   141→   141→  const [emailError, setEmailError] = useState('');
   142→   142→  const [isAuthenticated, setIsAuthenticated] = useState(false);
   143→   143→  const [loadingClientEmail, setLoadingClientEmail] = useState(true);
   144→   144→
   145→   145→  // Data states
   146→   146→  const [campaigns, setCampaigns] = useState<Campaign[]>([]);
   147→   147→  const [meetingNotes, setMeetingNotes] = useState<MeetingNote[]>([]);
   148→   148→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
   149→   149→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
   150→   150→
   151→   151→  const [clientContext, setClientContext] = useState<ClientContext | null>(null);
   152→   152→  const [linkedCRMAccount, setLinkedCRMAccount] = useState<{ scope: string | null; closed_at: string | null; qualified_at: string | null; created_at: string } | null>(null);
   153→   153→  const [decisionLog, setDecisionLog] = useState<DecisionLogEntry[]>([]);
   154→   154→  const [weeklyUpdates, setWeeklyUpdates] = useState<WeeklyUpdate[]>([]);
   155→   155→  const [showPreviousUpdates, setShowPreviousUpdates] = useState(false);
   156→   156→  const [hasOnboardingResponse, setHasOnboardingResponse] = useState<boolean | null>(null);
   157→   157→  const [onboardingFormSlug, setOnboardingFormSlug] = useState<string | null>(null);
   158→   158→  const [showWelcome, setShowWelcome] = useState(false);
   159→   159→  const [welcomePhase, setWelcomePhase] = useState<'enter' | 'ready' | 'opening' | 'done'>('enter');
   160→   160→  const [portalFadeIn, setPortalFadeIn] = useState(false);
   161→   161→
   162→   162→  // UI states
   163→   163→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'all' | 'active' | 'completed'>('all');
   164→   164→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
   165→   165→  const [viewingNote, setViewingNote] = useState<MeetingNote | null>(null);
   166→   166→
   167→   167→  // Cache key for this specific client portal
   168→   168→  const cacheKey = `portal_auth_${idOrSlug}`;
   169→   169→  const CACHE_DURATION = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours
   170→   170→
   171→   171→  // Resolve slug to UUID on mount
   172→   172→  useEffect(() => {
   173→   173→    async function resolveClientId() {
   174→   174→      try {
   175→   175→        if (isUUID(idOrSlug)) {
   176→   176→          setClientId(idOrSlug);
   177→   177→          // Fetch client email
   178→   178→          const { data, error } = await supabasePublic
   179→   179→            .from('clients')
   180→   180→            .select('id, name, email, slug, logo_url')
   181→   181→            .eq('id', idOrSlug)
   182→   182→            .is('archived_at', null)
   183→   183→            .single();
   184→   184→
   185→   185→          if (error || !data) {
   186→   186→            setError('Client not found');
   187→   187→            setLoadingClientEmail(false);
   188→   188→            return;
   189→   189→          }
   190→   190→          setClient(data);
   191→   191→          setClientEmail(data.email);
   192→   192→        } else {
   193→   193→          // Fetch by slug
   194→   194→          const { data, error } = await supabasePublic
   195→   195→            .from('clients')
   196→   196→            .select('id, name, email, slug, logo_url')
   197→   197→            .eq('slug', idOrSlug)
   198→   198→            .is('archived_at', null)
   199→   199→            .single();
   200→   200→
   201→   201→          if (error || !data) {
   202→   202→            setError('Client not found');
   203→   203→            setLoadingClientEmail(false);
   204→   204→            return;
   205→   205→          }
   206→   206→          setClientId(data.id);
   207→   207→          setClient(data);
   208→   208→          setClientEmail(data.email);
   209→   209→        }
   210→   210→      } catch (err) {
   211→   211→        setError('Failed to load client');
   212→   212→        setLoadingClientEmail(false);
   213→   213→      }
   214→   214→    }
   215→   215→    resolveClientId();
   216→   216→  }, [idOrSlug]);
   217→   217→
   218→   218→  // Check cached auth when client email is available
   219→   219→  useEffect(() => {
   220→   220→    if (clientEmail) {
   221→   221→      checkCachedAuth();
   222→   222→      setLoadingClientEmail(false);
   223→   223→    }
   224→   224→  }, [clientEmail]);
   225→   225→
   226→   226→  // Fetch data when authenticated
   227→   227→  useEffect(() => {
   228→   228→    if (isAuthenticated && clientId) {
   229→   229→      fetchCampaigns();
   230→   230→      fetchMeetingNotes();
   231→   231→      fetchClientContext();
   232→   232→      fetchDecisionLog();
   233→   233→      fetchWeeklyUpdates();
   234→   234→      checkOnboardingStatus();
   235→   235→    }
   236→   236→  }, [clientId, isAuthenticated]);
   237→   237→
   238→   238→  // Check if user is already authenticated via cache
   239→   239→  const checkCachedAuth = () => {
   240→   240→    if (!clientEmail) return;
   241→   241→
   242→   242→    try {
   243→   243→      const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey);
   244→   244→      if (cached) {
   245→   245→        const { email: cachedEmail, timestamp } = JSON.parse(cached);
   246→   246→        const now = Date.now();
   247→   247→
   248→   248→        if (now - timestamp < CACHE_DURATION) {
   249→   249→          if (cachedEmail && cachedEmail.toLowerCase() === clientEmail.toLowerCase()) {
   250→   250→            setEmail(cachedEmail);
   251→   251→            setIsAuthenticated(true); // Start loading data immediately
   252→   252→            setWelcomePhase('enter');
   253→   253→            setShowWelcome(true);
   254→   254→            requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   255→   255→              setTimeout(() => setWelcomePhase('ready'), 50);
   256→   256→            });
   257→   257→            return;
   258→   258→          }
   259→   259→        }
   260→   260→        localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);
   261→   261→      }
   262→   262→    } catch (error) {
   263→   263→      console.error('Error checking cached auth:', error);
   264→   264→      localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);
   265→   265→    }
   266→   266→  };
   267→   267→
   268→   268→  // Save authentication to cache
   269→   269→  const saveAuthToCache = (email: string) => {
   270→   270→    try {
   271→   271→      const authData = {
   272→   272→        email,
   273→   273→        clientId,
   274→   274→        timestamp: Date.now()
   275→   275→      };
   276→   276→      localStorage.setItem(cacheKey, JSON.stringify(authData));
   277→   277→      // Save global portal auth for cross-page navigation (campaign/report pages)
   278→   278→      const globalAuthData = {
   279→   279→        email,
   280→   280→        clientId,
   281→   281→        clientEmail,
   282→   282→        timestamp: Date.now()
   283→   283→      };
   284→   284→      localStorage.setItem('portal_global_auth', JSON.stringify(globalAuthData));
   285→   285→    } catch (error) {
   286→   286→      console.error('Error saving auth to cache:', error);
   287→   287→    }
   288→   288→  };
   289→   289→
   290→   290→  const handleEmailSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
   291→   291→    e.preventDefault();
   292→   292→    setEmailError('');
   293→   293→
   294→   294→    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
   295→   295→    if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
   296→   296→      setEmailError('Please enter a valid email address');
   297→   297→      return;
   298→   298→    }
   299→   299→
   300→   300→    if (!clientEmail) {
```
</details></tool-use>

   332→   332→  async function checkOnboardingStatus() {
   333→   333→    if (!clientId) return;

   335→   335→      // Find the onboarding form
   336→   336→      const { data: form } = await supabasePublic
   337→   337→        .from('forms')

   339→   339→        .eq('slug', 'holo-hive-onboarding')
   340→   340→        .eq('status', 'published')
   341→   341→        .single();
   342→   342→
   343→   343→      if (!form) {
   344→   344→        setHasOnboardingResponse(true); // no form exists, hide banner
   345→   345→        return;
   346→   346→      }
   347→   347→
   348→   348→      setOnboardingFormSlug(form.slug);
   349→   349→
   350→   350→      // Check if this client has already submitted a response
   351→   351→      const { count } = await supabasePublic
   352→   352→        .from('form_responses')
   353→   353→        .select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })
   354→   354→        .eq('form_id', form.id)
   355→   355→        .eq('client_id', clientId);
   356→   356→
   357→   357→      setHasOnboardingResponse((count || 0) > 0);
   358→   358→    } catch (err) {

   360→   360→      setHasOnboardingResponse(true); // hide banner on error
   361→   361→    }
   362→   362→  }
   363→   363→
   364→   364→  async function fetchCampaigns() {

   374→   374→          name,

   386→   386→            likes,
   387→   387→            comments,
   388→   388→            retweets,
   389→   389→            bookmarks
   390→   390→          )
   391→   391→        `)
   392→   392→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   393→   393→        .is('archived_at', null)

   395→   395→
   396→   396→      if (campaignsError) throw campaignsError;
   397→   397→
   398→   398→      const processedCampaigns = campaignsData?.map(campaign => {
   399→   399→        const contents = (campaign as any).contents || [];
   400→   400→        const totalImpressions = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) => sum + (c.impressions || 0), 0);
   401→   401→        const totalEngagement = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) =>
   402→   402→          sum + (c.likes || 0) + (c.comments || 0) + (c.retweets || 0) + (c.bookmarks || 0), 0);
   403→   403→
   404→   404→        return {
   405→   405→          ...campaign,
   406→   406→          kol_count: (campaign as any).campaign_kols?.[0]?.count || 0,
   407→   407→          content_count: contents.length,
   408→   408→          total_impressions: totalImpressions,
   409→   409→          total_engagement: totalEngagement,
   410→   410→        };
   411→   411→      }) || [];
   412→   412→
   413→   413→      setCampaigns(processedCampaigns as Campaign[]);
   414→   414→    } catch (err) {

   416→   416→      setError('Failed to load campaigns');

   766→   766→          <div className="absolute rounded-full" style={{ width: '400px', height: '400px', background: 'radial-gradient(circle, rgba(62,134,146,0.15) 0%, transparent 60%)', bottom: '-80px', left: '-80px' }} />

   786→   786→              />

   805→   805→                  required

   807→   807→                  style={{ background: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.08)', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12)' }}
   808→   808→                />

   816→   816→                style={{ background: 'linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(255,255,255,0.2) 0%, rgba(255,255,255,0.1) 100%)', border: '1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)', boxShadow: '0 2px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)' }}
   817→   817→              >

  1080→  1080→        {weeklyUpdates.length > 0 && (

  1083→  1083→              <div className="flex items-center gap-3">

  1089→  1089→              </div>
  1090→  1090→            </CardHeader>

  1103→  1103→                          <span>{item.replace(/^[-•]\s*/, '')}</span>

  1107→  1107→                  </div>

  1123→  1123→                {weeklyUpdates.length > 1 && (

  1127→  1127→                      className="flex items-center gap-1 text-sm text-[#3e8692] hover:text-[#2d6570] font-medium"
  1128→  1128→                    >
  1129→  1129→                      {showPreviousUpdates ? <ChevronUp className="h-4 w-4" /> : <ChevronDown className="h-4 w-4" />}
  1130→  1130→                      {showPreviousUpdates ? 'Hide' : 'Show'} Previous Updates ({weeklyUpdates.length - 1})
  1131→  1131→                    </button>
  1132→  1132→                    {showPreviousUpdates && (

  1159→  1159→            <div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:items-center sm:justify-between gap-4">

  1163→  1163→                <Input
  1164→  1164→                  placeholder="Search campaigns..."
  1165→  1165→                  value={searchTerm}

  1169→  1169→              </div>
  1170→  1170→            </div>

  1175→  1175→              <TabsList className="bg-gray-100 p-1 rounded-lg">
  1176→  1176→                <TabsTrigger value="all" className="rounded-md px-4 py-2 data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:shadow-sm cursor-pointer">All ({stats.total})</TabsTrigger>
  1177→  1177→                <TabsTrigger value="active" className="rounded-md px-4 py-2 data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:shadow-sm cursor-pointer">Active ({stats.active + stats.planning})</TabsTrigger>
  1178→  1178→                <TabsTrigger value="completed" className="rounded-md px-4 py-2 data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:shadow-sm cursor-pointer">Completed ({stats.completed})</TabsTrigger>
  1179→  1179→              </TabsList>
  1180→  1180→            </Tabs>
  1181→  1181→
  1182→  1182→            {/* Campaign List */}

  1185→  1185→                {[1, 2, 3].map(i => (

  1188→  1188→                  </div>
  1189→  1189→                ))}
  1190→  1190→              </div>
  1191→  1191→            ) : filteredCampaigns.length === 0 ? (
  1192→  1192→              <div className="text-center py-16">
  1193→  1193→                <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-gray-100 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mx-auto mb-4">
  1194→  1194→                  <Megaphone className="h-8 w-8 text-gray-400" />
  1195→  1195→                </div>
  1196→  1196→                <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-2">No campaigns found</h3>
  1197→  1197→                <p className="text-gray-500">
  1198→  1198→                  {searchTerm ? 'Try a different search term.' : 'No campaigns match the selected filter.'}
  1199→  1199→                </p>
  1200→  1200→              </div>

  1339→  1339→                          <h3 className="font-bold text-lg text-gray-900 truncate group-hover:text-[#3e8692] transition-colors">
  1340→  1340→                            {note.title}
  1341→  1341→                          </h3>
  1342→  1342→                          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-2 text-sm text-gray-500">
  1343→  1343→                            <div className="p-1 bg-gray-100 rounded">
  1344→  1344→                              <Calendar className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
  1345→  1345→                            </div>
  1346→  1346→                            <span>{new Date(note.meeting_date + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })}</span>
  1347→  1347→                          </div>
  1348→  1348→                        </div>
  1349→  1349→                        <Button
  1350→  1350→                          variant="outline"
  1351→  1351→                          size="sm"
  1352→  1352→                          className="rounded-lg border-gray-200 hover:border-[#3e8692] hover:text-[#3e8692] transition-colors flex-shrink-0 ml-4"
  1353→  1353→                          onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setViewingNote(note); }}
  1354→  1354→                        >
  1355→  1355→                          <ExternalLink className="h-4 w-4 mr-1.5" />
  1356→  1356→                          View Note
  1357→  1357→                        </Button>
  1358→  1358→                      </div>
  1359→  1359→                    </div>
  1360→  1360→                  </div>
  1361→  1361→                ))}
  1362→  1362→              </div>
  1363→  1363→            </CardContent>
  1364→  1364→          </Card>
  1365→  1365→        )}
  1366→  1366→
  1367→  1367→        {/* Meeting Note Detail Dialog */}
  1368→  1368→        <Dialog open={!!viewingNote} onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) setViewingNote(null); }}>
  1369→  1369→          <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[700px] max-h-[85vh] overflow-hidden border-l-4 border-l-[#3e8692] rounded-xl">
  1370→  1370→            <DialogHeader className="pb-4 border-b border-gray-100">
  1371→  1371→              <DialogTitle className="text-xl font-bold text-gray-900">{viewingNote?.title}</DialogTitle>
  1372→  1372→              <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-500 mt-1">
  1373→  1373→                <div className="p-1 bg-gray-100 rounded">
  1374→  1374→                  <Calendar className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
  1375→  1375→                </div>
  1376→  1376→                <span>
  1377→  1377→                  {viewingNote && new Date(viewingNote.meeting_date + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' })}
  1378→  1378→                </span>
  1379→  1379→              </div>
  1380→  1380→            </DialogHeader>
  1381→  1381→            <div className="overflow-y-auto max-h-[60vh] pr-2 pt-2 space-y-4">
  1382→  1382→              {viewingNote?.attendees && (
  1383→  1383→                <div className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-600">
  1384→  1384→                  <Users className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
  1385→  1385→                  <span><span className="font-medium">Attendees:</span> {viewingNote.attendees}</span>
  1386→  1386→                </div>
  1387→  1387→              )}
  1388→  1388→              {viewingNote?.content ? (
  1389→  1389→                <div className="ql-snow">
  1390→  1390→                  <div className="ql-editor !px-0 !text-gray-600" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: viewingNote.content }} />
  1391→  1391→                </div>
  1392→  1392→              ) : (
  1393→  1393→                <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">No content for this note.</p>
  1394→  1394→              )}
  1395→  1395→              {viewingNote?.action_items && (
  1396→  1396→                <div className="border-t border-gray-100 pt-3">
  1397→  1397→                  <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-700 mb-2">Action Items</p>
  1398→  1398→                  <div className="ql-snow">
  1399→  1399→                    <div className="ql-editor !px-0 !text-gray-600 !text-sm" dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: viewingNote.action_items }} />
  1400→  1400→                  </div>
  1401→  1401→                </div>
  1402→  1402→              )}
  1403→  1403→            </div>
  1404→  1404→          </DialogContent>
  1405→  1405→        </Dialog>
  1406→  1406→
  1407→  1407→        {/* Decision Log Section */}
  1408→  1408→        {decisionLog.length > 0 && (
  1409→  1409→          <Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mt-8">
  1410→  1410→            <CardHeader className="bg-white border-b border-gray-100 pb-4">
  1411→  1411→              <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
  1412→  1412→                <div className="p-2 bg-gradient-to-br from-purple-500 to-purple-600 rounded-xl shadow-lg">
  1413→  1413→                  <MessageSquare className="h-5 w-5 text-white" />
  1414→  1414→                </div>
  1415→  1415→                <CardTitle className="text-xl font-bold text-gray-900">Decision Log</CardTitle>
  1416→  1416→                <span className="text-sm text-gray-500">({decisionLog.length})</span>
  1417→  1417→              </div>
  1418→  1418→            </CardHeader>
  1419→  1419→            <CardContent className="p-6">
  1420→  1420→              <div className="space-y-3">
  1421→  1421→                {decisionLog.map((dec) => (
  1422→  1422→                  <div key={dec.id} className="flex items-start gap-4 border-l-4 border-l-purple-300 pl-4 py-2">
  1423→  1423→                    <div className="text-xs text-gray-400 whitespace-nowrap mt-0.5">
  1424→  1424→                      {new Date(dec.decision_date + 'T00:00:00').toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' })}
  1425→  1425→                    </div>
  1426→  1426→                    <p className="text-sm text-gray-700">{dec.summary}</p>
  1427→  1427→                  </div>
  1428→  1428→                ))}
  1429→  1429→              </div>
  1430→  1430→            </CardContent>
  1431→  1431→          </Card>
  1432→  1432→        )}
  1433→  1433→
  1434→  1434→        {/* Footer */}
  1435→  1435→        <div className="mt-12 pt-8 border-t border-gray-200">
  1436→  1436→          <div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-center justify-between gap-4 text-sm text-gray-500">
  1437→  1437→            <p>Need help? Contact your campaign manager or reach out to our team.</p>
  1438→  1438→            <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1439→  1439→              <span>Powered by</span>
  1440→  1440→              <Image
  1441→  1441→                src="/images/logo.png"
  1442→  1442→                alt="Holo Hive"
  1443→  1443→                width={80}
  1444→  1444→                height={24}
  1445→  1445→                className="h-5 w-auto opacity-60"
  1446→  1446→              />
  1447→  1447→            </div>
  1448→  1448→          </div>
  1449→  1449→        </div>
  1450→  1450→      </main>
  1451→  1451→    </div>
  1452→  1452→  );
  1453→  1453→}

   261→    if (!clientToShare) return '';
   262→    return `${typeof window !== 'undefined' ? window.location.origin : ''}/public/forms/${formSlugOrId}?client=${clientToShare.id}`;
   263→  };

   265→  const copyFormLink = (formSlugOrId: string, formName: string) => {
   266→    const url = getFormUrl(formSlugOrId);
   267→    if (!url) return;
   268→    navigator.clipboard.writeText(url);

   277→  useEffect(() => {
   278→    fetchClients();
   279→    if (userProfile?.role === 'admin' || userProfile?.role === 'super_admin') {
   280→      UserService.getAllUsers().then(setAllUsers);
   281→    }
   282→    fetchPartners();
   283→  }, [user?.id, userProfile?.role]);
   284→
   285→  // Handle partner filtering
   286→  useEffect(() => {
   287→    if (partnerIdParam) {
   288→      const partner = allPartners.find(p => p.id === partnerIdParam);
   289→      setFilteredPartnerName(partner?.name || null);
   290→    } else {
   291→      setFilteredPartnerName(null);
   292→    }
   293→  }, [partnerIdParam, allPartners]);
   294→  const fetchPartners = async () => {
   295→    try {
   296→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   297→        .from('partners')
   298→        .select('id, name')
   299→        .eq('is_active', true)
   300→        .order('name');
   301→
   302→      if (error) throw error;
   303→      setAllPartners(data || []);
   304→    } catch (err) {
   305→      console.error('Error fetching partners:', err);
   306→    }
   307→  };
   308→
   309→  const fetchClients = async () => {
   310→    if (!user?.id || !userProfile?.role) return;
   311→    try {
   312→      setLoading(true);
   313→      setError(null);
   314→      const fetchedClients = await ClientService.getClientsForUser(
   315→        userProfile.role as 'admin' | 'member' | 'client',
   316→        user.id
   317→      );
   318→
   319→      setClients(fetchedClients);
   320→      // Fetch campaigns for all clients and compute status breakdown
   321→      const clientIds = fetchedClients.map(c => c.id);
   322→      const allCampaigns: any[] = await CampaignService.getCampaignsByClientIds(clientIds);
   323→      const statusMap: Record<string, Record<CampaignStatus, number>> = {};
   324→      for (const client of fetchedClients) {
   325→        statusMap[client.id] = { Planning: 0, Active: 0, Paused: 0, Completed: 0 };
   326→      }
   327→      for (const campaign of allCampaigns) {
   328→        if (statusMap[campaign.client_id]) {
   329→          statusMap[campaign.client_id][campaign.status as CampaignStatus]++;
   330→        }
   331→      }
   332→      setClientsWithStatus(
   333→        fetchedClients.map(client => ({
   334→          ...client,
   335→          campaignsByStatus: statusMap[client.id],
   336→        }))
   337→      );
   338→
   339→      // Fetch linked accounts (opportunities with client_id)
   340→      const allOpportunities = await CRMService.getAllOpportunities();
   341→      const accountsMap: Record<string, CRMOpportunity[]> = {};
   342→      for (const opp of allOpportunities) {
   343→        if (opp.client_id) {
   344→          if (!accountsMap[opp.client_id]) {
   345→            accountsMap[opp.client_id] = [];
   346→          }
   347→          accountsMap[opp.client_id].push(opp);
   348→        }
   349→      }

   443→      action_items: note.action_items || null,
   444→      created_by: user?.id,
   445→    });

   451→    const { error } = await supabase.from('client_meeting_notes').update({
   452→      title: note.title,

   455→      attendees: note.attendees || null,

   459→    if (error) throw error;
   460→    await refreshMeetingNotes();
   461→  };
   462→
   463→  const deleteMeetingNote = async (noteId: string) => {

   480→
   481→  // Parse Gemini meeting notes .docx
   482→  const handleDocUpload = async (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
   483→    const file = e.target.files?.[0];
   484→    if (!file) return;
   485→    // Reset input so the same file can be re-uploaded
   486→    e.target.value = '';
   487→
   488→    if (!file.name.endsWith('.docx')) {
   489→      toast({ title: 'Invalid file', description: 'Please upload a .docx file (Gemini meeting notes export).', variant: 'destructive' });
   490→      return;
   491→    }
   492→
   493→    setIsParsingDoc(true);
   494→    try {
   495→      const arrayBuffer = await file.arrayBuffer();
   496→      const result = await mammoth.extractRawText({ arrayBuffer });
   497→      const text = result.value;
   498→      const lines = text.split('\n').map(l => l.trim()).filter(l => l.length > 0);
   499→
   500→      // Parse Gemini meeting notes format
   501→      let title = '';
   502→      let meetingDate: Date | undefined;
   503→      let attendees = '';
   504→      let content = '';
   505→      let actionItems = '';
   506→
   507→      // Line 1: Date (e.g. "Feb 10, 2026")
   508→      // Line 2: Title
   509→      // Line starting with "Invited": attendees
   510→      // After "Summary" or "Details": content
   511→      // After "Suggested next steps": action items
   512→
   513→      let section = 'header'; // header | summary | details | actions | footer
   514→      let lineIndex = 0;
   515→
   516→      for (const line of lines) {
   517→        // Skip footer lines from Gemini
   518→        if (line.startsWith('You should review Gemini') || line.startsWith('Please provide feedback')) continue;
   519→        if (line.startsWith('Get tips and learn how Gemini')) continue;
   520→
   521→        if (lineIndex === 0) {
   522→          // First line is the date
   523→          const parsed = new Date(line);
   524→          if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) {
   525→            meetingDate = parsed;
   526→          }
   527→          lineIndex++;
   528→          continue;
   529→        }
   530→
   531→        if (lineIndex === 1) {
   532→          title = line;
   533→          lineIndex++;
   534→          continue;
   535→        }
   536→
   537→        if (line.startsWith('Invited ')) {
   538→          attendees = line.replace('Invited ', '').trim();
   539→          lineIndex++;
   540→          continue;
   541→        }
   542→
   543→        if (line.startsWith('Attachments ')) {
   544→          lineIndex++;
   545→          continue;
   546→        }
   547→
   548→        if (line === 'Summary') {
   549→          section = 'summary';
   550→          lineIndex++;
   551→          continue;
   552→        }
   553→
   554→        if (line === 'Details') {
   555→          section = 'details';
   556→          lineIndex++;
   557→          continue;
   558→        }
   559→
   560→        if (line === 'Suggested next steps') {
   561→          section = 'actions';
   562→          lineIndex++;
   563→          continue;
   564→        }
   565→
   566→        if (section === 'summary' || section === 'details') {
   567→          content += (content ? '\n\n' : '') + line;
   568→        } else if (section === 'actions') {
   569→          actionItems += (actionItems ? '\n' : '') + line;
```
</details></tool-use>

I now have all the information I need to produce a comprehensive implementation plan. Let me compile it.

---

## Implementation Plan: Three Portal Features

Here is the complete, detailed implementation plan for all three features. I'll address each feature independently, then cover cross-cutting concerns (migration, RLS, admin side changes) at the end.

---

### Codebase Summary (What I Found)

**Portal page** (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`): 1,454 lines, `'use client'`, uses `supabasePublic` (anon client), 8 existing data-fetch `async function`s, structured as a single large component. The page renders in order: Header, Welcome, Onboarding Banner, Client Context, Stats Cards, Weekly Status, Campaigns section, Meeting Notes, Decision Log, Footer.

**`client_context` columns confirmed:** `id, client_id, engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts, created_at, updated_at` — no resource columns yet.

**RLS analysis:**
- `campaign_kols`: has `public_campaign_kols_anon_select` policy (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT already works.
- `contents`: has `public_contents_anon_select` policy (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT already works.
- `master_kols`: has `public_master_kols_anon_select` + `Public can view shared master KOLs` (`qual: true`) — anon SELECT works.
- `client_context`: has `Anon users can select client_context` (`qual: true`) — works.
- `form_responses`: has `Clients can check their own responses` (`qual: client_id IS NOT NULL`) — anon CAN read because the policy has no `auth.uid()` check, just requires the column not be null. This works as-is.
- `form_fields`: has `Anyone can view published form fields` policy which requires the form to have `status = 'published'` — the `holo-hive-onboarding` form IS published, so anon can already read fields. No new policy needed.
- `forms`: has `Anyone can view published forms` (`status = 'published'`) — works.

**Onboarding form fields confirmed:** 22 fields across 7 pages. Labels contain HTML (inline styles, bold tags). Need to strip HTML when displaying. The answer-bearing field types are: `text`, `textarea`, `select`. `section` and `description` type fields have no corresponding `response_data` values and must be skipped. Field IDs are the keys in `response_data` JSONB.

**Admin context edit form** is a Dialog in `app/clients/page.tsx` using `handleContextSubmit()` which does update/insert to `client_context`. The `contextForm` state must be extended to include resource fields.

---

### Feature 1: KOL Roster

#### Data Fetch Strategy

The Supabase query chain is: `campaigns` (for this `client_id`) → `campaign_kols` (for each `campaign_id`, filter `hidden != true`) → join to `master_kols` (via `master_kol_id`) → join to `contents` (via `campaign_kols_id`).

The most efficient single query uses Supabase's nested select with an explicit or-filter for the `hidden` column:

```typescript
async function fetchKolRoster() {
  if (!clientId) return;
  try {
    // Step 1: Get all campaigns for this client
    const { data: campaignsData } = await supabasePublic
      .from('campaigns')
      .select('id, name, status')
      .eq('client_id', clientId)
      .is('archived_at', null)
      .order('start_date', { ascending: false });

    if (!campaignsData?.length) {
      setKolRoster([]);
      return;
    }

    const campaignIds = campaignsData.map(c => c.id);

    // Step 2: Fetch campaign_kols with nested master_kols and contents
    const { data: kolsData } = await supabasePublic
      .from('campaign_kols')
      .select(`
        id,
        campaign_id,
        hh_status,
        client_status,
        allocated_budget,
        hidden,
        master_kols (
          id,
          name,
          link,
          platform,
          followers,
          tier,
          niche,
          region,
          content_type,
          creator_type
        ),
        contents (
          id,
          platform,
          type,
          status,
          content_link,
          activation_date,
          impressions,
          likes,
          retweets,
          comments,
          bookmarks
        )
      `)
      .in('campaign_id', campaignIds)
      .or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false');

    // Step 3: Group by campaign
    const rosterByCampaign = campaignsData.map(campaign => ({
      campaign,
      kols: (kolsData || []).filter(k => k.campaign_id === campaign.id)
    })).filter(g => g.kols.length > 0);

    setKolRoster(rosterByCampaign);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error fetching KOL roster:', err);
  }
}
```

#### New Types to Add

```typescript
type KolRosterEntry = {
  id: string;                    // campaign_kols.id
  campaign_id: string;
  hh_status: string | null;
  client_status: string | null;
  allocated_budget: number | null;
  master_kols: {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    link: string | null;
    platform: string[] | null;
    followers: number | null;
    tier: string | null;
    niche: string[] | null;
    region: string | null;
    content_type: string[] | null;
    creator_type: string[] | null;
  } | null;
  contents: {
    id: string;
    platform: string | null;
    type: string | null;
    status: string | null;
    content_link: string | null;
    activation_date: string | null;
    impressions: number | null;
    likes: number | null;
    retweets: number | null;
    comments: number | null;
    bookmarks: number | null;
  }[];
};

type KolRosterGroup = {
  campaign: { id: string; name: string; status: string };
  kols: KolRosterEntry[];
};
```

#### Status Mapping

```typescript
const HH_STATUS_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {
  'Curated': 'Shortlisted',
  'Contacted': 'Pitching',
  'Interested': 'Negotiating',
  'Onboarded': 'Content Creation',
  'Concluded': 'Completed',
};
```

#### Status Badge Colors

Match the existing portal color system:
- Shortlisted → blue-100/blue-800
- Pitching → yellow-100/yellow-800
- Negotiating → orange-100/orange-800
- Content Creation → purple-100/purple-800
- Completed → gray-100/gray-800

#### New State

```typescript
const [kolRoster, setKolRoster] = useState<KolRosterGroup[]>([]);
const [kolRosterLoading, setKolRosterLoading] = useState(false);
```

#### Call Site

Add `fetchKolRoster()` to the existing `useEffect` that fires when `isAuthenticated && clientId`.

#### Render Location

After the Campaigns `</Card>` closing tag and before Meeting Notes. The section is always rendered (if `kolRoster.length > 0`).

#### UI Structure

```
<Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mt-8">
  <CardHeader>
    <Users icon> "KOL Roster" <span>({total count})</span>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    {kolRoster.map(group => (
      <div key={group.campaign.id}>
        {/* Campaign header */}
        <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-3 mt-6 first:mt-0">
          <Badge status-color>{group.campaign.status}</Badge>
          <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900">{group.campaign.name}</h3>
          <span className="text-sm text-gray-400">({group.kols.length} KOLs)</span>
        </div>
        {/* Table */}
        <div className="overflow-x-auto">
          <table className="w-full text-sm">
            <thead>
              <tr className="text-xs text-gray-500 uppercase border-b">
                <th>KOL</th><th>Platform</th><th>Tier</th>
                <th>Status</th><th>Content</th><th>Impressions</th><th>Engagement</th>
              </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
              {group.kols.map(kol => {
                const totalImpressions = kol.contents.reduce(...)
                const totalEngagement = kol.contents.reduce(...)
                const liveLinks = kol.contents.filter(c => c.content_link)
                return (
                  <tr key={kol.id} className="border-b last:border-0 hover:bg-gray-50">
                    <td>
                      {kol.master_kols?.link
                        ? <a href={kol.master_kols.link} target="_blank">{kol.master_kols?.name}<ExternalLink/></a>
                        : kol.master_kols?.name}
                    </td>
                    <td>{platforms as badges}</td>
                    <td><Badge>{kol.master_kols?.tier}</Badge></td>
                    <td><Badge>{HH_STATUS_LABELS[kol.hh_status] || kol.hh_status}</Badge></td>
                    <td>
                      {liveLinks.length > 0
                        ? liveLinks.map(c => <a href={c.content_link} target="_blank"><ExternalLink/></a>)
                        : <span className="text-gray-400">{kol.contents.length > 0 ? kol.contents.length + ' pieces' : '—'}</span>}
                    </td>
                    <td>{formatNumber(totalImpressions) || '—'}</td>
                    <td>{formatNumber(totalEngagement) || '—'}</td>
                  </tr>
                )
              })}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        </div>
      </div>
    ))}
  </CardContent>
</Card>
```

#### New Icons Needed

Add to the existing import from `lucide-react`: `Link2` (for live content links). `Users` is already imported.

---

### Feature 2: Campaign DNA

#### Data Fetch Strategy

Three sequential fetches (form fields can be fetched in parallel with the response):

```typescript
async function fetchCampaignDNA() {
  if (!clientId) return;
  try {
    // 1. Get the onboarding form (already fetched in checkOnboardingStatus, reuse form id)
    // The form id '911f79b1-...' can be looked up by slug if not cached

    // 2. Fetch form fields AND form response in parallel
    const [fieldsResult, responseResult] = await Promise.all([
      supabasePublic
        .from('form_fields')
        .select('id, label, field_type, display_order, page_number')
        .eq('form_id', onboardingFormId)
        .not('field_type', 'in', '("section","description")')
        .order('page_number')
        .order('display_order'),
      supabasePublic
        .from('form_responses')
        .select('response_data, submitted_at')
        .eq('form_id', onboardingFormId)
        .eq('client_id', clientId)
        .order('submitted_at', { ascending: false })
        .limit(1)
        .single()
    ]);

    if (!responseResult.data) return; // no submission yet

    setDnaFields(fieldsResult.data || []);
    setDnaResponse(responseResult.data);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error fetching campaign DNA:', err);
  }
}
```

**Key note on timing:** `checkOnboardingStatus()` already fetches the form id. The easiest approach is to store the form id in state (not just the slug), then `fetchCampaignDNA()` depends on that id. Alternatively, accept the redundant form lookup inside `fetchCampaignDNA`.

#### HTML Label Stripping

Form field labels contain inline HTML (font tags, span style attributes). A small utility is needed:

```typescript
const stripHtml = (html: string): string => {
  // Use DOM parser in client context, or regex for simple case
  return html.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '').replace(/&amp;/g, '&').replace(/&quot;/g, '"').trim();
};

// Extract the clean question label: "1. Key Objectives: What are your main goals?"
// (labels contain number prefix and bold formatting)
```

#### Page-to-Section Grouping

The 7 form pages map to readable section names:
- Page 1: (intro/section only, no answer fields — already filtered out by `not field_type in (section, description)`)
- Page 2: "Project Vision" (fields: Key Objectives, Success Metrics, Elevator Pitch)
- Page 3: "Team & Timeline" (fields: Team Roles, Decision-Making, Milestones)
- Page 4: "Details & Assets" (fields: Tokenomics, Brand Assets, Materials, Project Links)
- Page 5: "Rewards" (field: Incentive Systems)
- Page 6: "Compliance" (field: Regulatory Compliance)
- Page 7: "Final Check" (field: Anything else)

Group by `page_number` when rendering.

#### New Types

```typescript
type DnaField = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  field_type: string;
  display_order: number;
  page_number: number;
};

type DnaResponse = {
  response_data: Record<string, any>;
  submitted_at: string | null;
};
```

#### New State

```typescript
const [dnaFields, setDnaFields] = useState<DnaField[]>([]);
const [dnaResponse, setDnaResponse] = useState<DnaResponse | null>(null);
const [onboardingFormId, setOnboardingFormId] = useState<string | null>(null);
```

Update `checkOnboardingStatus()` to also `setOnboardingFormId(form.id)`.

#### Render Location

After the Engagement Overview (Client Context) `</Card>` and before Stats Cards. Condition: `hasOnboardingResponse === true && dnaResponse !== null`.

#### UI Structure

```
<Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mb-10">
  <CardHeader>
    <FileText icon> "Campaign Brief"
    <span className="ml-auto text-sm text-gray-400">
      Submitted {formatDate(dnaResponse.submitted_at)}
    </span>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    {/* Group fields by page_number */}
    {groupedPages.map(page => {
      const answeredFields = page.fields.filter(f => dnaResponse.response_data[f.id]);
      if (!answeredFields.length) return null;
      return (
        <div key={page.pageNumber} className="mb-6 last:mb-0">
          <h4 className="text-xs font-semibold text-gray-400 uppercase tracking-wider mb-3">
            {PAGE_SECTION_NAMES[page.pageNumber] || `Section ${page.pageNumber}`}
          </h4>
          <div className="space-y-4">
            {answeredFields.map(field => (
              <div key={field.id}>
                <p className="text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 mb-1">
                  {stripHtml(field.label)}
                </p>
                <p className="text-sm text-gray-800 whitespace-pre-wrap leading-relaxed">
                  {String(dnaResponse.response_data[field.id])}
                </p>
              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
          {/* Divider between sections */}
        </div>
      );
    })}
  </CardContent>
</Card>
```

#### RLS Confirmation

- `forms`: `Anyone can view published forms` — works (holo-hive-onboarding is published).
- `form_fields`: `Anyone can view published form fields` — works (linked form is published).
- `form_responses`: `Clients can check their own responses` with `qual: client_id IS NOT NULL` — this policy applies to ALL roles including anon, and requires only that `client_id` column is not null. Since the portal correctly passes `.eq('client_id', clientId)`, this will match rows where `client_id = clientId`. This works as-is with no new policy needed.

---

### Feature 3: Resource Vault

#### Database Migration

Add three individual columns to `client_context` (your recommended approach):

```sql
-- Migration: add_resource_columns_to_client_context
ALTER TABLE client_context
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS telegram_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS shared_drive_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gtm_sync_url TEXT;

COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.telegram_url IS 'Telegram group invite link for this client';
COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.shared_drive_url IS 'Shared Google Drive or similar link';
COMMENT ON COLUMN client_context.gtm_sync_url IS 'GTM sync / Notion / campaign tracker URL';
```

File name: `supabase/migrations/20260312_add_resource_columns_to_client_context.sql`

No new RLS policy needed — `client_context` already has `Anon users can select client_context` (`qual: true`).

#### Portal Side

**New state:**

The `ClientContext` type already drives the portal display. Since `fetchClientContext()` selects specific columns, add the three new columns to both the TypeScript type and the `.select()` call:

```typescript
// Type update
type ClientContext = {
  id: string;
  engagement_type: string | null;
  scope: string | null;
  start_date: string | null;
  milestones: string | null;
  client_contacts: string | null;
  holohive_contacts: string | null;
  // New:
  telegram_url: string | null;
  shared_drive_url: string | null;
  gtm_sync_url: string | null;
};
```

```typescript
// fetchClientContext() select update
.select('id, engagement_type, scope, start_date, milestones, client_contacts, holohive_contacts, telegram_url, shared_drive_url, gtm_sync_url')
```

No new fetch function — the resources come from the same `client_context` row.

#### Render Location

At the very bottom, after the Decision Log card and before the Footer. Condition: `clientContext && (clientContext.telegram_url || clientContext.shared_drive_url || clientContext.gtm_sync_url)`.

#### Resource Link Config

```typescript
const RESOURCE_LINKS = [
  {
    key: 'telegram_url' as const,
    label: 'Telegram Group',
    icon: MessageSquare,  // already imported
    color: 'from-blue-500 to-blue-600',
  },
  {
    key: 'shared_drive_url' as const,
    label: 'Shared Drive',
    icon: FileText,       // already imported
    color: 'from-green-500 to-green-600',
  },
  {
    key: 'gtm_sync_url' as const,
    label: 'Campaign Tracker',
    icon: BarChart3,      // already imported
    color: 'from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6570]',
  },
];
```

All three icons are already imported in the portal page.

#### UI Structure

```
{clientContext && (clientContext.telegram_url || clientContext.shared_drive_url || clientContext.gtm_sync_url) && (
  <Card className="border-0 shadow-lg rounded-xl overflow-hidden mt-8">
    <CardHeader className="bg-white border-b border-gray-100 pb-4">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
        <div className="p-2 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6570] rounded-xl shadow-lg">
          <ExternalLink className="h-5 w-5 text-white" />
        </div>
        <CardTitle className="text-xl font-bold text-gray-900">Resources</CardTitle>
      </div>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent className="p-6">
      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-4">
        {RESOURCE_LINKS.filter(r => clientContext[r.key]).map(resource => (
          <a
            key={resource.key}
            href={clientContext[resource.key]!}
            target="_blank"
            rel="noopener noreferrer"
            className="group flex items-center gap-3 p-4 rounded-xl border border-gray-100 
                       bg-white hover:shadow-md hover:-translate-y-0.5 transition-all duration-300"
          >
            <div className={`p-2.5 bg-gradient-to-br ${resource.color} rounded-xl shadow-sm flex-shrink-0`}>
              <resource.icon className="h-5 w-5 text-white" />
            </div>
            <div className="min-w-0">
              <p className="font-medium text-gray-900 text-sm">{resource.label}</p>
              <p className="text-xs text-gray-400 truncate group-hover:text-[#3e8692] transition-colors">
                Open link
              </p>
            </div>
            <ExternalLink className="h-4 w-4 text-gray-300 group-hover:text-[#3e8692] ml-auto transition-colors flex-shrink-0" />
          </a>
        ))}
      </div>
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
)}
```

#### Admin Side: clients/page.tsx

Three changes needed:

**1. Update the `ClientContext` type** in `clients/page.tsx` (same type addition as portal):
```typescript
type ClientContext = {
  // ... existing fields ...
  telegram_url: string | null;
  shared_drive_url: string | null;
  gtm_sync_url: string | null;
};
```

**2. Extend `contextForm` state** in the `useState` initializer:
```typescript
const [contextForm, setContextForm] = useState<{
  // ... existing fields ...
  telegram_url: string;
  shared_drive_url: string;
  gtm_sync_url: string;
}>({
  // ... existing defaults ...
  telegram_url: '',
  shared_drive_url: '',
  gtm_sync_url: '',
});
```

**3. Populate `contextForm` in `openContextModal()`** (around line 655):
```typescript
telegram_url: ctx?.telegram_url || '',
shared_drive_url: ctx?.shared_drive_url || '',
gtm_sync_url: ctx?.gtm_sync_url || '',
```

**4. Include in `handleContextSubmit()` payload** (around line 668):
```typescript
telegram_url: contextForm.telegram_url || null,
shared_drive_url: contextForm.shared_drive_url || null,
gtm_sync_url: contextForm.gtm_sync_url || null,
```

**5. Add three Input fields** at the bottom of the Context Modal's form `<div>` (after the Holo Hive Contacts field, before `</div>`):
```tsx
<div className="grid gap-2">
  <Label>Telegram Group URL</Label>
  <Input value={contextForm.telegram_url} onChange={(e) => setContextForm({ ...contextForm, telegram_url: e.target.value })} placeholder="https://t.me/..." className="auth-input" />
</div>
<div className="grid gap-2">
  <Label>Shared Drive URL</Label>
  <Input value={contextForm.shared_drive_url} onChange={(e) => setContextForm({ ...contextForm, shared_drive_url: e.target.value })} placeholder="https://drive.google.com/..." className="auth-input" />
</div>
<div className="grid gap-2">
  <Label>Campaign Tracker URL</Label>
  <Input value={contextForm.gtm_sync_url} onChange={(e) => setContextForm({ ...contextForm, gtm_sync_url: e.target.value })} placeholder="https://..." className="auth-input" />
</div>
```

---

### Implementation Sequence

Follow this order to avoid blocking issues:

**Step 1 — Database migration** (must be first):
Run the SQL migration to add the three columns to `client_context`. Everything else depends on this for Feature 3.

**Step 2 — Feature 3: Resource Vault** (simplest, good warm-up):
- Update `ClientContext` type in both files.
- Update `contextForm` state and `handleContextSubmit` in `clients/page.tsx`.
- Add fields to the Context Modal UI in `clients/page.tsx`.
- Update `fetchClientContext()` select in portal page.
- Add Resources card to portal JSX.

**Step 3 — Feature 2: Campaign DNA** (medium complexity):
- Add `onboardingFormId` state, update `checkOnboardingStatus` to store it.
- Add `fetchCampaignDNA()` function.
- Add `dnaFields`, `dnaResponse` state.
- Add `stripHtml` utility function.
- Add page-section name map.
- Add Campaign Brief card to portal JSX, placed after the Engagement Overview card.

**Step 4 — Feature 1: KOL Roster** (most complex):
- Add `KolRosterEntry`, `KolRosterGroup` types.
- Add `kolRoster`, `kolRosterLoading` state.
- Add `fetchKolRoster()` function.
- Add `HH_STATUS_LABELS` map and `getKolStatusBadge()` helper.
- Add `Link2` to lucide-react imports.
- Add KOL Roster card to portal JSX after the Campaigns card.

---

### Edge Cases and Gotchas

**Feature 1 — KOL Roster:**
- The `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')` filter must be applied as a PostgREST OR filter: `.or('hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false')` at the `campaign_kols` query level.
- If a client has many campaigns (e.g., 20+) with many KOLs each, the `campaign_ids` array in `.in('campaign_id', campaignIds)` could be large. This is fine for typical client scales but worth noting.
- `master_kols` relation: Supabase nested select using `master_kols (...)` will return the object directly (not an array) since `master_kol_id` is a single FK. TypeScript type should be `master_kols: { ... } | null` not an array.
- `contents` relation: returns an array via `campaign_kols_id` FK. Already confirmed by column schema.
- KOLs with `client_status = 'Rejected'` are not explicitly hidden but may be confusing to clients. Since you haven't specified filtering them, leave them visible — but if needed, add `.neq('client_status', 'Rejected')` to the query.

**Feature 2 — Campaign DNA:**
- Form field labels are HTML strings — `stripHtml` is essential. The regex approach `html.replace(/<[^>]*>/g, '')` is safe here since there's no untrusted content, just the admin's own HTML styling.
- Some fields may have empty/null values in `response_data`. Filter with `if (!dnaResponse.response_data[field.id]) continue`.
- The `response_data` keys are field UUIDs. The map from field id → label is built by indexing `dnaFields` by `id`.
- `select` type fields (e.g., Decision-Making, Incentive Systems) may store their answer as a string value — render it as plain text.
- The `fetchCampaignDNA()` call depends on `onboardingFormId` being set. It should be called inside `checkOnboardingStatus` after form ID is known, or triggered via a `useEffect` dependency on `onboardingFormId`.

**Feature 3 — Resource Vault:**
- All three new columns are nullable. The portal card must only render when at least one has a value — the condition handles this.
- URL validation is not needed on display; if an admin enters a bad URL it simply opens a broken link.
- The `refreshClientContexts()` function in `clients/page.tsx` uses `select('*')` so it will automatically include new columns after migration.

---

### Files to Modify Summary

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` | Add types, state, 3 fetch functions, 3 new card sections |
| `app/clients/page.tsx` | Extend `ClientContext` type, `contextForm` state, `handleContextSubmit`, `openContextModal`, Context Modal UI |
| New migration SQL file | `ALTER TABLE client_context ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS telegram_url TEXT, shared_drive_url TEXT, gtm_sync_url TEXT` |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` - Core file receiving all 3 feature additions; contains all existing fetch patterns, state management, and JSX layout to follow
- `app/clients/page.tsx` - Admin side for Resource Vault; contains the `ClientContext` type definition and the Context Modal form that needs new fields (around lines 49-61 for type, lines 130 and 665-688 for form logic, lines 2647-2724 for modal UI)
- `supabase/migrations/20260312_add_resource_columns_to_client_context.sql` - New migration file (following the pattern of `20250121_add_client_logo.sql`) to add the three resource URL columns to `client_context` before any code changes are deployed
- `lib/clientService.ts` - Reference for understanding how the authenticated Supabase client patterns differ from the portal's `supabasePublic` anon client; no modifications needed but useful context for ensuring queries are issued correctly against the anon client

```md
# Client Portal: KOL Roster, Campaign DNA, Resource Vault

## Context
The client portal currently shows campaigns, weekly updates, meeting notes, and engagement overview. Three new features will make it a more complete "command center":
1. **KOL Roster** — interactive table of KOLs across all campaigns, with status and metrics
2. **Campaign DNA** — parsed onboarding form responses displayed as a clean brief
3. **Resource Vault** — persistent links (Telegram, shared drive, tracker)

## Files to Modify
- `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` — all 3 features (types, state, fetch, render)
- `app/clients/page.tsx` — Resource Vault admin fields in Context Modal (~lines 130, 644-675, 2712-2716)
- DB migration via Supabase MCP — add 3 columns to `client_context`

## Step 1: DB Migration (Resource Vault)
Add to `client_context`:
\```sql
ALTER TABLE client_context
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS telegram_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS shared_drive_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gtm_sync_url TEXT;
\```
No new RLS needed — existing anon SELECT policy covers it.

## Step 2: Resource Vault

### Admin side (`app/clients/page.tsx`)
- Extend `contextForm` useState (~line 130) with `telegram_url: string`, `shared_drive_url: string`, `gtm_sync_url: string`
- Populate in `openContextModal` (~line 644) from existing context data
- Include in `handleContextSubmit` payload (~line 670)
- Add 3 Input fields after Holo Hive Contacts (~line 2716) in the Context Modal

### Portal side (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`)
- Update `ClientContext` type to include the 3 new fields
- Update `fetchClientContext` select string to include them
- Render a "Resources" card at the bottom (after Decision Log) with icon cards for each non-null link
- Only show if at least one resource URL exists

## Step 3: Campaign DNA

### Portal (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`)
- Store `onboardingFormId` in state (set during existing `checkOnboardingStatus`)
- New `fetchCampaignDNA()`: fetch `form_fields` (filtered to exclude section/description types) + `form_responses` for this client
- `stripHtml()` utility to clean HTML from field labels
- Render "Campaign Brief" card after Engagement Overview, before Stats Cards
- Group fields by `page_number`, show label → answer pairs
- Only render if onboarding response exists

### RLS: All needed policies already exist (forms/form_fields readable by anon when published, form_responses readable by anon when client_id is not null)

## Step 4: KOL Roster

### Portal (`app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx`)
- New `fetchKolRoster()`:
  1. Get campaign IDs for this client
  2. Fetch `campaign_kols` (filtered `hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false`) with nested `master_kols(name, link, platform, tier, followers)` and `contents(content_link, impressions, likes, comments, retweets, bookmarks)`
  3. Group by campaign
- Status mapping: Curated→"Shortlisted", Contacted→"Pitching", Interested→"Negotiating", Onboarded→"Content Creation", Concluded→"Completed"
- Color-coded status badges
- Render after Campaigns section, before Meeting Notes
- Table per campaign: KOL name (linked), platforms, tier, status, content links, impressions, engagement
- Only show if any KOLs exist

### RLS: All needed policies already exist (campaign_kols, master_kols, contents all have anon SELECT)

## Portal Section Order (after changes)
1. Header
2. Welcome text
3. Onboarding Banner (if form not submitted)
4. Engagement Overview
5. **Campaign Brief** ← NEW (if form submitted)
6. Stats Cards
7. Weekly Status
8. Campaigns list
9. **KOL Roster** ← NEW
10. Meeting Notes
11. Decision Log
12. **Resources** ← NEW

## Verification
1. Add resource URLs via client Context Modal → they appear in portal Resource Vault
2. Submit onboarding form for a client → Campaign Brief card appears with parsed answers
3. Campaigns with KOLs → KOL Roster shows grouped by campaign with correct statuses
4. Empty states: no resources = no card, no form response = no DNA card, no KOLs = no roster

```

    62→2. Dialog: "Deal Won!" with two options
    63→3. Create new: name pre-filled, enter email → creates client, sets `client_id`

  1. Get campaign IDs for this client
  2. Fetch `campaign_kols` (filter `hidden.is.null,hidden.eq.false`) with nested `master_kols(name, link, platform, tier)` and `contents(content_link, impressions, likes, comments, retweets, bookmarks)`
  3. Group by campaign
- Status mapping: Curated→"Shortlisted", Contacted→"Pitching", Interested→"Negotiating", Onboarded→"Content Creation", Concluded→"Completed"
- Color-coded status badges
- Render after Campaigns section, before Meeting Notes
- Table per campaign group: KOL name (linked), platforms, tier, status, live links, impressions, engagement

## Portal Section Order (final)
1. Header
2. Welcome text
3. Onboarding Banner (if form not submitted)
4. Engagement Overview
5. **Campaign Brief** ← NEW (if form submitted)
6. Stats Cards
7. Weekly Status
8. Campaigns list
9. **KOL Roster** ← NEW
10. Meeting Notes
11. Decision Log
12. **Resources** ← NEW

## Verification
1. Add resource URLs via client Context Modal → appear in portal Resource Vault
2. Submit onboarding form → Campaign Brief card shows parsed answers
3. Campaigns with KOLs → KOL Roster shows grouped by campaign
4. Empty states: no resources/response/KOLs = respective cards don't render
